member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsExternalInit
member.summary: Reserved to be used by the compiler for tracking metadata. This class should not be used by developers in source code.
member.remarks: This definition is provided by the IsExternalInit NuGet package (https://www.nuget.org/packages/IsExternalInit). Please see https://github.com/manuelroemer/IsExternalInit for more information.

members.member:
MARK: RemoveAllParties
Removes all parties in the provided list.

MARK: RemoveRandomItems
Randomly selects and removes items from a list using the provided removal function.
The type of items in the list The list of items to select from How many items to randomly remove Function that removes all items in a list and returns count removed Number of items actually removed

MARK: RemoveHideouts
Removes all hideouts in the provided list by destroying all bandit parties inside them.

MARK: GetHideoutCulture
Gets the culture of bandits in the hideout. If hideout has no parties, returns terrain-appropriate culture instead of null.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Bandits.BanditManager.CreateBanditParty(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Hideout,TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CultureObject,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates bandit party of the specified culture at the specified hideout

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Bandits.BanditManager.CreateBanditParty(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Hideout)
member.summary: Create a bandit party at a hideout using the apropiate bandit culture for the hideout. If no boss party exists at hideout, the party will be a boss party which will never leave the hideout.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Bandits.BanditManager.ActivateHideout(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Hideout,TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CultureObject)
member.summary: Activates a hideout by creating parties for the hideout until atleast 3 bandit parties are inside the hideout. A hideout is considered activated if two or more bandit parties are inside the hideout, but bandit parties will not leave the hideout until there is more than three parties as three parties always stay in hideout. If the hideout doesnt have a boss party, a boss party will be created. If the hideout is already active no regular parties will be created.
member.param: The hideout to activate

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Banners.KingdomColorResult
member.summary: Result of kingdom color selection containing the chosen color ID and the minimum perceptual distance to the nearest existing kingdom color.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Banners.BannerColorPicker
member.summary: Provides methods for selecting banner colors from the game's palette and finding complementary colors for banner design.

members.member:
MARK: GetRandomColorID
Gets a random color ID from the banner palette.
A valid color ID that exists in the palette.

MARK: GetLighterColor
Finds a lighter complementary color from the palette based on the given color ID.
The base color ID to find a lighter complement for. Minimum brightness difference (0.0 to 1.0). Default is 0.15. A color ID for a lighter color, or the base color if no suitable lighter color exists.

MARK: GetDarkerColor
Finds a darker complementary color from the palette based on the given color ID.
The base color ID to find a darker complement for. Minimum brightness difference (0.0 to 1.0). Default is 0.15. A color ID for a darker color, or the base color if no suitable darker color exists.

MARK: GetContrastingColor
Finds a contrasting color from the palette that provides maximum visibility against the base color. Prefers colors with high luminance difference and complementary hues for better emblem visibility.
The base color ID to find a contrasting color for. If true, returns a lighter color; if false, returns a darker color. Minimum brightness difference (0.0 to 1.0). Default is 0.3 for stronger contrast. A color ID for a contrasting color, or the base color if no suitable contrasting color exists.

MARK: GetColorScheme
Gets a complete banner color scheme with main background, secondary background, and emblem colors. Automatically selects the best theme (standard or alternative) based on the main color's luminance. For lighter main colors, uses darker secondary and emblem. For darker main colors, uses lighter emblem.
Output: The main background color ID. Output: The secondary background color ID. Output: The emblem color ID (high contrast).

MARK: GetColorScheme
Gets a complete banner color scheme with main background, secondary background, and emblem colors for a specific main color. Automatically selects the best theme based on the main color's luminance. For lighter main colors (luminance > 0.6), uses lighter secondary and darker emblem. For darker main colors (luminance <= 0.6), uses darker secondary and lighter emblem.
The main background color ID to use. Output: The secondary background color ID. Output: The emblem color ID (high contrast).

MARK: GetAlternativeScheme
Gets an alternative banner color scheme with lighter secondary background and darker emblem. Automatically selects a random main color and applies the alternative theme. Best for creating banners with lighter overall appearance.
Output: The main background color ID. Output: The secondary background color ID (lighter). Output: The emblem color ID (darker, high contrast).

MARK: GetAlternativeScheme
Gets an alternative banner color scheme with lighter secondary background and darker emblem for a specific main color. Always uses the alternative theme regardless of luminance. Best for creating banners with lighter overall appearance.
The main background color ID to use. Output: The secondary background color ID (lighter). Output: The emblem color ID (darker, high contrast).

MARK: GetStandardScheme
Gets a standard banner color scheme with darker secondary background and lighter emblem for a specific main color. Always uses the standard theme regardless of luminance. Best for creating banners with darker overall appearance.
The main background color ID to use. Output: The secondary background color ID (darker). Output: The emblem color ID (lighter, high contrast).

MARK: CalculateLuminance
Calculates the relative luminance of a color using the standard formula.
The uint color value in ARGB format. Luminance value between 0.0 (black) and 1.0 (white).

MARK: CalculateHueDiff
Calculates hue similarity between two colors (0 = same hue, 180 = opposite).

MARK: GetHue
Gets the hue of a color in degrees (0-360).

MARK: GetSaturation
Gets the saturation of a color (0-1).

MARK: PerceptualDistance
Calculates a saturation-aware perceptual distance between two colors. Dynamically adjusts hue weight based on the minimum saturation of both colors, so that desaturated colors (grays/creams) are compared primarily by luminance rather than by their meaningless hue values.
First color as uint value. Second color as uint value. Perceptual distance between 0.0 (identical) and ~1.0 (maximally different).

MARK: ComplementaryColor
Finds the best complementary color from a list of candidates. Prefers colors with similar hue but different brightness, avoiding harsh contrasts.

MARK: ContrastingColor
Finds the best contrasting color from a list of candidates. Prefers colors with complementary hues and high saturation for maximum visibility.

MARK: GetColorInfo
Gets color information for debugging purposes.
The color ID to get information for. A string containing color information, or null if the color doesn't exist.

MARK: AreColorsSimilar
Determines if two colors are similar based on perceptual difference. Uses saturation-aware perceptual distance that dynamically adjusts hue weight based on color saturation, properly handling desaturated colors.
First color ID from banner palette. Second color ID from banner palette. Perceptual similarity threshold (0.0-1.0). Lower values mean more strict matching. Default is 0.2. True if colors are perceptually similar, false otherwise.

MARK: AreColorsSimilar
Determines if two colors (by uint value) are similar based on perceptual difference. Uses saturation-aware perceptual distance that dynamically adjusts hue weight based on color saturation, properly handling desaturated colors.
First color as uint value. Second color as uint value. Perceptual similarity threshold (0.0-1.0). Lower values mean more strict matching. Default is 0.2. True if colors are perceptually similar, false otherwise.

MARK: GetUniqueClanColor
Gets the most unique color ID from the banner palette compared to existing clans. Ensures the selected color is perceptually distinct from all existing clan colors.
Minimum perceptual difference threshold (0.0-1.0). Default is 0.15 for good distinction. A banner palette color ID that is maximally distinct from existing clan colors.

MARK: GetUniqueKingdomColor
Gets the most unique color ID from the banner palette compared to existing kingdoms using deterministic max-min distance scored selection. Scores every palette color by its minimum perceptual distance to all active kingdom colors, applies an adaptive saturation floor to prefer vivid colors, and picks from the top candidates.
A containing the chosen color ID and its minimum distance to the nearest kingdom.

MARK: ScoreAndPick
Scores palette colors by minimum perceptual distance to existing kingdom colors and returns the best candidate using weighted random selection from the top 3-5.

MARK: AdaptiveSatFloor
Calculates an adaptive saturation floor based on kingdom count pressure. At low kingdom counts, enforces vivid colors only. As color space fills, gradually relaxes to allow more muted tones.
Number of non-eliminated kingdoms. Total number of palette colors available. Minimum saturation value (0.0 to 0.25).

MARK: UniqueKingdomIconColor
Gets a unique icon color for a kingdom banner using a dual-strategy approach. Below the pressure threshold (~12 kingdoms): focuses on aesthetic harmony and visibility against the banner background using the existing contrasting color system. Above the pressure threshold: focuses on icon uniqueness from kingdoms with similar background shades while maintaining minimum visibility.
The kingdom's background (primary) color ID. The minimum perceptual distance from the background to the nearest kingdom color. A palette color ID for the icon color.

MARK: PickWeightedTop
Picks an index from the top of a sorted list using weighted randomness. 70% chance for index 0, 20% for index 1, 10% for index 2. Clamps to available count.
Total number of available items in the sorted list. Selected index (0, 1, or 2).

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Banners.BannerEditorController.OpenBannerEditor(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Clan,System.Boolean,System.Action)
member.summary: Opens the native banner editor for any clan banner.
member.param: The clan whose banner to edit.
When true, strips the banner to a single centered icon before opening the editor. On cancel, the original multi-icon banner is fully restored.
Optional callback invoked when the editor state finalizes.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Banners.BannerExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for Banner objects.

members.member:
MARK: SetAllIconColorIds
Sets the icon color for ALL icon layers in the banner (indices 1..N). Fixes the native SetIconColorId() which only updates index 1.
The banner to update. The palette color ID to apply to all icon layers.

MARK: ApplyRandomScheme
Applies a random color scheme to the banner with high-contrast emblem. Automatically selects the best theme based on the randomly chosen main color's luminance. For lighter main colors, uses lighter secondary and darker emblem. For darker main colors, uses darker secondary and lighter emblem.
The banner to apply colors to. The banner instance for method chaining.

MARK: ApplyColorScheme
Applies a color scheme to the banner using a specific primary color with high-contrast emblem. Automatically selects the best theme based on the provided main color's luminance. For lighter main colors (luminance > 0.6), uses lighter secondary and darker emblem. For darker main colors (luminance <= 0.6), uses darker secondary and lighter emblem.
The banner to apply colors to. The primary (main background) color ID to use. The banner instance for method chaining.

MARK: ApplyAltColorScheme
Applies an alternative random color scheme to the banner with high-contrast emblem. Always uses lighter secondary background and darker emblem regardless of main color luminance. Best for creating banners with lighter overall appearance.
The banner to apply colors to. The banner instance for method chaining.

MARK: ApplyAltColorScheme
Applies an alternative color scheme to the banner using a specific primary color with high-contrast emblem. Always uses lighter secondary background and darker emblem regardless of main color luminance. Best for creating banners with lighter overall appearance.
The banner to apply colors to. The primary (main background) color ID to use. The banner instance for method chaining.

MARK: ApplyStandardScheme
Applies a standard color scheme to the banner using a specific primary color with high-contrast emblem. Always uses darker secondary background and lighter emblem regardless of main color luminance. Best for creating banners with darker overall appearance.
The banner to apply colors to. The primary (main background) color ID to use. The banner instance for method chaining.

MARK: ApplyUniqueScheme
Applies a unique color scheme to the banner that is distinct from existing clan colors. Automatically finds a unique primary color from the banner palette that differs from all existing clans, then applies the best theme (standard or alternative) based on that color's luminance. Ideal for creating new clans with visually distinct banners.
The banner to apply colors to. Minimum perceptual difference from existing clans (0.0-1.0). Default is 0.15 for good distinction. The banner instance for method chaining.

MARK: ApplyUniqueKingdomScheme
Applies a unique color scheme to the banner that is distinct from existing kingdom background colors. Uses deterministic max-min distance scored selection for the background color, adaptive saturation floor to prefer vivid colors, and pressure-aware icon color selection that switches between aesthetic harmony (low kingdom count) and uniqueness (high kingdom count).
The banner to apply colors to. The banner instance for method chaining.

MARK: ConvertToSingleIcon
Strips all icon layers except the last one (the main/primary icon). The last icon is typically the primary/main icon that sits on top of all others. Does not modify icon position or rotation -- use ResetIconTransforms() for that.
The banner to convert. The original serialized banner code before stripping, or null if the banner already had only one icon (or no icons).

MARK: ResetIconTransforms
Resets position to center (764, 764) and rotation to 0 for ALL icon layers. Useful for fixing rotated or off-center icons. Does not strip any icons -- use ConvertToSingleIcon() for that. 764 = BannerFullSize / 2 = 1528 / 2.
The banner to reset transforms on. The banner instance for method chaining.

MARK: RebuildWithoutStroke
Rebuilds icon layers that have DrawStroke=true as new BannerData objects with DrawStroke=false, preserving all other properties (MeshId, Size, Position, ColorId, Mirror, Rotation). Sets ColorId2=ColorId to prevent the stroke-same-as-icon-color blurring artifact. Uses serialize-deserialize cycle to create fresh BannerData objects and invalidate the visual cache (_bannerVisual=null forces regeneration on next access).
The banner to rebuild. The banner instance for method chaining.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Banners.BannerPaletteExpander
member.summary: Temporarily unlocks all 229 banner palette colors for the banner editor by setting PlayerCanChooseForSigil and PlayerCanChooseForBackground to true on every BannerColor entry. Backs up the original palette for restoration when the editor closes.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Banners.BannerPaletteExpander.ExpandAllColors
member.summary: Expands the banner color palette so all colors are available for both sigil and background selection. Must be called before constructing BannerEditorVM so RefreshValues() sees the expanded palette.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Banners.BannerPaletteExpander.RestoreOriginalColors
member.summary: Restores the original banner color palette after the editor closes. Prevents the expanded palette from leaking into other game systems.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Banners.ClanBannerEditorState.GetOriginalBannerCode
member.summary: Returns the serialized banner code from before icon stripping, or null if no stripping was performed. Used to restore the original multi-icon banner on cancel.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Banners.UI.ClanBannerEditorScreen.SetClanPartyVisualsAsDirty(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Clan)
member.summary: Marks all party visuals for the target clan as dirty so map icons refresh with new banner colors. Follows the native SetMapIconAsDirtyForAllPlayerClanParties pattern but works for any clan. Calls both SetVisualAsDirty() and SetNavalVisualAsDirty() on each party (matching native pattern).

member.summary: Allows BLGMObjectManager to Initialize if new game

member.summary: Allows BLGMObjectManager to Initialize if new game

member.summary: Initalize BLGMObjectManager and load existing objects

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.EquipmentRepairBehavior
member.summary:
Detects BLGM heroes with trash or missing equipment after save load and re-equips them using HeroEquipper.
Fires on OnGameLoadFinishedEvent which runs after the native CheckInvalidEquipmentsAndReplaceIfNeeded() has already replaced !IsReady items with DefaultItems.Trash or cleared slots.

members.member:
MARK: OnGameLoadFinished
Scans all BLGM heroes for damaged equipment and re-equips them. Runs once after save load is fully complete.

MARK: RepairBlgmHeroEquipment
Iterates all BLGM-tracked heroes, detects equipment damage from save load, and re-equips affected heroes using HeroEquipper.EquipHeroByStats(). Also removes DefaultItems.Trash from party inventories.

MARK: HeroNeedsEquipmentRepair
Determines if a hero's equipment was damaged by the native trash replacement. Checks for DefaultItems.Trash and !IsReady items only. Empty slots are intentional and should not trigger repair.
The hero to check. True if equipment needs repair; false otherwise.

MARK: HasDamagedEquipment
Checks an equipment set for trash items or items with IsReady = false.
The equipment to check. True if any slot contains damaged equipment.

MARK: RemoveTrashFromInventory
Removes all DefaultItems.Trash from a hero's party inventory. The native trash replacement adds trash items to the party ItemRoster.
The hero whose party inventory to clean.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementCultureBehavior
member.summary: Campaign behavior that manages custom settlement cultures with save/load persistence. Stores both custom cultures and original cultures to allow reset functionality. Uses parallel lists for reliable serialization with TaleWorlds save system.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementCultureBehavior.OnSessionLaunched(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CampaignGameStarter)
member.summary: Called after session is loaded. Reapplies all custom settlement cultures.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementCultureBehavior.SetSettlementCulture(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement,TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CultureObject,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Changes the culture of a settlement and tracks the change for save persistence.
member.param: The settlement to change culture for
The new culture to apply
If true, updates the culture of all notables in the settlement
If true and settlement is a town, recursively updates bound villages
member.returns: True if successful, false otherwise

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementCultureBehavior.ResetSettlementCulture(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement)
member.summary: Resets a settlement to its original culture.
member.param: The settlement to reset
member.returns: True if reset, false if settlement culture was not changed

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementCultureBehavior.HasCustomCulture(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement)
member.summary: Checks if a settlement has a custom culture.
member.param: The settlement to check
member.returns: True if the settlement has a custom culture

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementCultureBehavior.GetOriginalCulture(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement)
member.summary: Gets the original culture of a settlement if it was changed.
member.param: The settlement to check
member.returns: Original culture if changed, null otherwise

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementCultureBehavior.GetCustomCultureCount
member.summary: Gets the number of settlements with custom cultures.
member.returns: The count of settlements with custom cultures

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementCultureBehavior.ApplyCultureChange(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement,TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CultureObject,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Applies the raw culture mutation to a settlement and optionally its notables and bound villages. This is the low-level operation with no persistence tracking. Used by both the load path (OnSessionLaunched) and the user action path (SetSettlementCulture).
member.param: The settlement to change
The culture to apply
If true, updates notables' cultures
If true, updates bound villages' cultures

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementNameBehavior
member.summary: Campaign behavior that manages custom settlement names with save/load persistence. This is the central handler for all settlement rename operations. Stores both custom names and original names to allow reset functionality. Uses parallel lists for reliable serialization with TaleWorlds save system.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementNameBehavior.OnSessionLaunched(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CampaignGameStarter)
member.summary: Called after session is loaded. Reapplies all custom settlement names. Uses ApplyNameOnly to avoid re-saving already persisted data.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementNameBehavior.ApplyNameOnly(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement,System.String)
member.summary: Internal method that applies a name change via reflection. Does NOT persist - used for on-load restoration and by RenameSettlement. Note: The map nameplate will update automatically when the player opens a menu. The hover tooltip updates immediately.
member.param: The settlement to rename
The new name for the settlement
member.returns: BLGMResult indicating success or failure with a message

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Behaviours.SettlementNameBehavior.RenameSettlement(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement,System.String)

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Characters.CharacterQueries
member.summary: Provides query methods for searching, finding, filtering, or sorting CharacterObject entities. This is a COMPREHENSIVE query - returns ALL CharacterObjects by default (heroes, troops, templates, NPCs). Use CharacterTypes flags to filter specific types. Use TroopQueries for filtered combat troops only.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Characters.CharacterQueries.GetCharacterById(System.String)
member.summary:
Finds a character with the specified characterId, Fast but case sensitive (all string ids SHOULD be lower case)
Use QueryCharacterObjects().FirstOrDefault() to find character with case insensitive partial name or partial stringIds
Example: QueryCharacterObjects("imperial").FirstOrDefault()

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Characters.CharacterQueries.QueryCharacterObjects(System.String,Bannerlord.GameMaster.Characters.CharacterTypes,System.Boolean,System.Collections.Generic.List{TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CultureObject},System.Int32,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Performance focused method to find CharacterObjects matching multiple parameters. All parameters are optional and can be used with none, one or a combination of any parameters
By default returns EVERYTHING (heroes, troops, templates, NPCs, etc.) Use CharacterTypes flags to filter specific types.
Note: parameter is a string used that will match partial character names or partial stringIds
member.param: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter by name or ID
CharacterTypes flags to filter by
If true, character must have ALL flags. If false, character must have ANY flag
Optional list of CultureObjects to filter by
Optional tier filter (0-6+, -1 for no filter)
Sort field (id, name, tier, level, culture, formation, or any CharacterTypes flag)
True for descending, false for ascending
member.returns: List of CharacterObjects matching all criteria

member.summary: Check if character matches all filter criteria

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Characters.CharacterTemplatePooler.GetAllHeroTemplatesFromFlags(Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureFlags,Bannerlord.GameMaster.Characters.GenderFlags)
member.summary: Gets character objects suitable for ALL hero creation (lords, wanderers, companions). Returns ONLY Lord and Wanderer occupations (both templates and actual characters). Excludes ALL notable occupations (Headman, RuralNotable, Artisan, Merchant, etc.) to prevent crashes. Excludes dead heroes to avoid using deceased character appearances. Provides maximum character variety, especially for female characters.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Characters.CharacterTypes
member.summary: Flags enum for categorizing ALL CharacterObject types across multiple dimensions. Unlike TroopTypes which is focused on combat troops only, CharacterTypes covers EVERYTHING: heroes, troops, templates, NPCs, etc.

member.summary: Helpers to set private fields that are needed for clan creation to match native patterns

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Clans.ClanCreationHelpers.SetDistanceCacheDirty(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Clan)
member.summary: This sets a flag to ensures the clan's distance calculations are recalculated on first access.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Clans.ClanCreationHelpers.SetOriginalBannerColors(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Clan,TaleWorlds.Core.Banner)
member.summary: Sets the clan's saved banner color properties (BannerBackgroundColorPrimary/Secondary, BannerIconColor) using the public Clan.UpdateBannerColor() method. These are used by native UpdateBannerColorsAccordingToKingdom() to reconstruct colors on kingdom join/leave state transitions. Note: UpdateBannerColor sets Primary = Secondary = backgroundColor (matches native rebel clan creation pattern).

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Clans.ClanCreationHelpers.SetClanMidSettlement(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Clan,TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement)
member.summary:
Sets the private _midSettlement field on a clan using reflection. This is necessary to prevent crashes when the game's AI tries to access FactionMidSettlement.
DEPRECATED: Use clan.SetInitialHomeSettlement(settlement) and then call clan.CalculateMidSettlement() instead

members.member:
MARK: SetStringName
Renames clan using a string instead of TextObject

Mark: Details and Wrapper
Returns a formatted string containing the clan's details

MARK: UpdateBannerColorsForKingdom
Updates this clan's banner colors to match its kingdom's PrimaryBannerColor and SecondaryBannerColor. Replicates the native private Clan.UpdateBannerColorsAccordingToKingdom() logic using public Banner methods.
The clan whose banner colors should be updated. BLGMResult indicating success or failure.

MARK: IsRulingClan
Checks if this clan is the ruling clan of its kingdom.
True if the clan is the ruling clan of a kingdom, false otherwise.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Clans.ClanExtensionsWrapper
member.summary: Wrapper class implementing IEntityExtensions interface for Clan entities

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Clans.ClanGenerator
member.summary: Provides functionality for creating and configuring clans with customizable options. Uses the new HeroGenerator architecture for reliable hero creation.

members.member:
MARK: CreateNobleClan
Create a new clan with the specified name. The clan will have a party created for its leader filled with troops and companions.
Optional specify a hero to be moved to the clan and be its leader, defaults to null. A new hero will be created if hero is null
Optionally assign clan to kingdom, Kingdom defaults to null (Independent)
Name of clan (if null, generates random name from culture) Hero to be moved to clan and assigned as it's leader (if null, creates new hero) Optional: Kingdom to assign clan to, defaults to null (Independent) If true, creates a party for the clan leader (default: true) Number of companions to add to leader's party (default: 2, 0 to skip) Culture pool for leader creation if leader is null (default: AllMainCultures) clan tier that also controls clan gold, influence, and leader party strength (default: -1 = Random tier of 3, 4, or 5) The created clan

MARK: GenerateClans
Generate multiple clans with random names from culture lists. Uses the new hero creation architecture to prevent crashes from state conflicts.
Number of clans to generate Culture pool to select from (default: AllMainCultures) Optional kingdom for all clans to join (default: null/Independent) If true, creates parties for clan leaders (default: true) Number of companions per clan (default: 2) List of created clans

MARK: CreateMinorClan
Creates a minor faction clan (not a noble house). Useful for creating mercenary companies, bandit factions, or other minor groups.
Name of the minor clan (if null, generates random name) Optional leader hero (creates one if null) Culture for the clan (default: AllMainCultures) If true, creates a party for the leader (default: true) Number of companions to add to leader's party (default: 0) clan tier that also controls clan gold, influence, and leader party strength (default: -1 = Random tier of 1, 2, or 3) The created minor clan

MARK: CreateBaseClan
Creates the base clan with all common initialization. Type-specific configuration (troops, kingdom, finalization) handled by caller.

MARK: InitializeClanObject
Creates new Clan instance with MBGUID and temporary name to prevent crashes

MARK: CreateOrPrepareLeade
Creates new leader or prepares existing hero for clan leadership. ConfigureClanAndLeader handles cleanup, so no need to do it here.

MARK: SetClanNameRegister
Sets clan name (from parameter or generates from culture) and registers with game systems

MARK: PopulateParty
Populates leader's party with mixed-tier troops scaled to clan tier and optionally companions

MARK: ConfigureClan
Configure clan culture, troop and prepares Hero as clan leader

MARK: SetClanHomeSettlem
Sets Clan and Leader's Home Settlement and then calculate mid settlement

MARK: CreateClanBanner
Creates and assigns a random banner to clan. Banner uses smart 3 color complimentary scheme. clan.Color = primary background, clan.Color2 = icon color (matches native clan.Color2 semantics used by kingdom propagation for cloth tinting and banner icon color).

MARK: FinalizeClan
Sets clan Tier, Then gold and influence based on tier, Marks clan ready and dispatches clan created event

MARK: InitalizeRelations
Randomize relations for clan between other clans

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Clans.ClanQueries
member.summary: Provides query methods for searching, finding, filtering, or sorting clan entities.

members.member:
MARK: GetClanByID
Finds a clan with the specified clanId, Fast but case sensitive (all string ids SHOULD be lower case)
Use QueryClans().FirstOrDefault() to find clan with case insensitive partial name or partial stringIds
Example: QueryClans("vlandia").FirstOrDefault()

MARK: GetRandomMainCultureClan
Returns a random non-eliminated clan from any main culture.

MARK: GetRandomClan
Returns a random non-eliminated clan matching the specified culture. If no culture is specified, returns a random clan from any main culture.
Optional culture to filter by. If null, filters to main cultures only. A random matching clan, or null if none found.

MARK: QueryClans
Performance focused method to find clans matching multiple parameters. All parameters are optional and can be used with none, one or a combination of any parameters
Note: parameter is a string used that will match partial clan names or partial stringIds
Optional case-insensitive substring to filter by name or ID Clan type flags that ALL must match (AND logic) If true, clan must have ALL flags. If false, clan must have ANY flag Sort field (id, name, tier, gold, renown, kingdom, leader, heroes, or any ClanType flag) True for descending, false for ascending MBReadOnlyList of clans matching all criteria

MARK: MatchesFIlters
Check if clan matches all filter criteria

MARK: GetClanComparer
Get comparer for clan sorting

MARK: ParseClanType
Parse a string into ClanTypes enum value

MARK: ParseClanTypes
Parse multiple strings and combine into ClanTypes flags

MARK: GetFormattedDetails
Returns a formatted string listing clan details with aligned columns

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.BLGMResult
member.summary: An object containing a bool indicating if an operation succeeded, a string message with details of the result of the operation, and an exception if an exception occured. Also includes convenience methods for logging to game rgl log and system console and or displaying in game.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.BLGMResult.PrintToGameConsole(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Prints to the game console

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1
member.summary: Abstract base class used for different result types. Contains a bool indicating if an operation succeeded, a string message with details of the result of the operation, and an exception if an exception occured. Also includes convenience methods for logging to game rgl log and system console and or displaying in game.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.IsSuccess
member.summary: Indicates if operation succeded or failed

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.Message
member.summary: Message with details of operation result

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.Exception
member.summary: Exception details for logging

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.Prefix
member.summary: Prefix used to indicate result type in Logs(Override this)

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.Success(System.String)
member.summary: Factory Method for creating a success result

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.Error(System.String)
member.summary: Factory Method for creating a non exception error result

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.Error(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Factory Method for creating an esception error result

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.#ctor
member.summary: Creates an unhandled failure result, recommended to use one of the other constructors

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Used for successful results or results that failed without an exception

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Used for results in exception handling blocks

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.DisplayMessage
member.summary:
Displays a game log message using InformationManager.DisplayMessage(). Only displays contents of Prefix and Message. Does not display exception details by default
Message is red if wasSuccessful = false, green if wasSuccessful = true
member.returns: Returns itself to allow chaining

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.Log
member.summary: Writes to the game's main rgl log file the message and success status if exception occured. Will also always write to system console if console is attached. Automatically writes exception details if applicable. Does not write to custom log file, that is handled by CommandExecutor.Run()
member.returns: Returns itself to allow chaining

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.GetFormattedMessage
member.summary:
Returns the message formatted with prefix and if failure, also prefixed with "Error". Does not contain exception details automatically
Example: "[PREFIX] Error: result message" OR "[PREFIX] result message"

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.GetFormattedLogEntry
member.summary: Gets a formatted string for logging, includes exception details if available. Not meant for direct display to user.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Common.ResultBase`1.DisplayAndLog
member.summary: Displays message in game and writes to game's main log file. Will also write to system console if console is attached. Automatically logs exception details if applicable, but does not display exception details in game.
member.returns: Returns itself to allow chaining

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Common.Parsing.ArgumentParser
member.summary: Static class providing argument parsing utilities for console commands. Handles Bannerlord's console quirk where double quotes are stripped but single quotes are preserved. This is the single entry point for all argument parsing operations.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Common.Parsing.ArgumentParser.ParseQuotedArguments(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String})
member.summary: Parses arguments to properly handle quoted strings using SINGLE QUOTES. TaleWorlds removes double quotes but preserves single quotes. Use 'text with spaces' for multi-word arguments.
member.remarks:
Examples: Input: ["'vladiv", "castle'", "'castle", "of", "stone'", "example"] Output: ["vladiv castle", "castle of stone", "example"]
Input: ["'Castle", "of", "Rocks'"] Output: ["Castle of Rocks"]
Input: ["name:'Sir", "Galahad'", "count:5"] Output: ["name:Sir Galahad", "count:5"]
member.param: Raw arguments from Bannerlord console
member.returns: Processed arguments with quoted strings combined

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Common.Parsing.ArgumentParser.JoinRemainingArgs(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String},System.Int32)
member.summary: Joins remaining arguments starting from the specified index into a single string. Useful for commands that accept multi-word text as the last parameter.
member.param: Argument list
Index to start joining from
member.returns: Joined string or empty string if index out of range
member.example: JoinRemainingArgs(args, 2) for "gm.cmd arg1 arg2 word1 word2 word3" returns "word1 word2 word3"

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Common.Parsing.ArgumentParser.GetArgument(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String},System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets an argument at the specified index, or returns all remaining arguments joined if consumeRemaining is true. This is useful for parameters that should accept multi-word input.
member.param: Argument list
Index of the argument to get
If true, joins all args from index onwards
member.returns: Argument value or empty string if index out of range

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Common.Parsing.ArgumentParser.ParseArguments(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String})
member.summary: Parses arguments with support for both quoted strings and named arguments. First handles quoted arguments, then parses named arguments. This is the primary entry point for command argument parsing.
member.param: Raw arguments from Bannerlord console
member.returns: ParsedArguments instance with separated named and positional arguments
member.example: Input: ["count:5", "name:'Sir", "Galahad'", "vlandia"] Result: Named: {count: "5", name: "Sir Galahad"}, Positional: ["vlandia"]

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Common.Parsing.FlagParser

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.CultureQueryCommands.QueryCultureCommand
member.summary: List all cultures with optional filtering Usage: gm.query.culture [search terms] [main|bandit] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.culture Example: gm.query.culture main Example: gm.query.culture empire sort:name Example: gm.query.culture bandit sort:name:desc

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.CultureQueryCommands.QueryCultureInfoCommand
member.summary: Get detailed info about a specific culture by ID Usage: gm.query.culture_info <cultureId> Example: gm.query.culture_info empire Example: gm.query.culture_info vlandia

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.HeroQueryCommands.QueryHeroAnyCommand
member.summary: Find heroes matching ANY of the specified types (OR logic) Usage: gm.query.hero_any [search terms] [type keywords] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.hero_any lord wanderer (finds anyone who is lord OR wanderer) Example: gm.query.hero_any lord wanderer sort:name:desc

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.HeroQueryCommands.QueryHeroCommand
member.summary: Unified hero listing command with AND logic for type filters Usage: gm.query.hero [search terms] [type keywords] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.hero john lord female clanleader Example: gm.query.hero aserai wanderer sort:name Example: gm.query.hero dead kingdomruler sort:age:desc Example: gm.query.hero sort:wanderer (sorts by wanderer flag)

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.HeroQueryCommands.QueryHeroInfoCommand
member.summary: Get detailed info about a specific hero by ID Usage: gm.query.hero_info [heroId]

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.ItemModifierQueryCommands.QueryModifierInfoCommand
member.summary: Get detailed information about a specific modifier Usage: gm.query.modifier_info <modifier_name> Example: gm.query.modifier_info fine

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.ItemModifierQueryCommands.QueryModifiersCommand
member.summary: List all available item modifiers Usage: gm.query.modifiers [search] Example: gm.query.modifiers fine

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.ItemQueryCommands.QueryItemAnyCommand
member.summary: Find items matching ANY of the specified types (OR logic) Usage: gm.query.item_any [search terms] [type keywords] [tier keywords] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.item_any weapon armor (finds anything that is weapon OR armor) Example: gm.query.item_any bow crossbow tier5 sort:value

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.ItemQueryCommands.QueryItemCommand
member.summary: Unified item listing command with AND logic for type filters Usage: gm.query.item [search terms] [type keywords] [tier keywords] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.item sword weapon 1h Example: gm.query.item imperial armor tier3 Example: gm.query.item food sort:value:desc Example: gm.query.item bow ranged tier4 sort:name

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.ItemQueryCommands.QueryItemInfoCommand
member.summary: Get detailed info about a specific item by ID Usage: gm.query.item_info itemId

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.KingdomQueryCommands.QueryKingdomAnyCommand
member.summary: Find kingdoms matching ANY of the specified types (OR logic) Usage: gm.query.kingdom_any [search terms] [type keywords] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.kingdom_any atwar eliminated sort:name:desc

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.KingdomQueryCommands.QueryKingdomCommand
member.summary: Unified kingdom listing command with AND logic for type filters Usage: gm.query.kingdom [search terms] [type keywords] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.kingdom empire atwar Example: gm.query.kingdom eliminated sort:name Example: gm.query.kingdom sort:strength:desc Example: gm.query.kingdom sort:atwar (sorts by atwar flag)

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.KingdomQueryCommands.QueryKingdomInfoCommand
member.summary: Get detailed info about a specific kingdom by ID Usage: gm.query.kingdom_info <kingdomId>

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.SettlementQueryCommands.QuerySettlementAnyCommand
member.summary: Find settlements matching ANY of the specified types (OR logic) Usage: gm.query.settlement_any [search terms] [type keywords] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.settlement_any castle city (finds castles OR cities) Example: gm.query.settlement_any empire vlandia sort:name

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.SettlementQueryCommands.QuerySettlementCommand
member.summary: Unified settlement listing command with AND logic for type filters Usage: gm.query.settlement [search terms] [type keywords] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.settlement castle empire Example: gm.query.settlement pen city sort:prosperity:desc Example: gm.query.settlement player town sort:name

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.SettlementQueryCommands.QuerySettlementInfoCommand
member.summary: Get detailed info about a specific settlement by ID Usage: gm.query.settlement_info [settlementId] Example: gm.query.settlement_info town_empire_1

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.TroopQueryCommands.QueryCharacterObjectsAnyCommand
member.summary: Query ALL CharacterObjects matching ANY of the specified types (OR logic). Returns EVERYTHING by default (heroes, troops, templates, NPCs). Use type keywords to filter specific categories. Usage: gm.query.character_objects_any [search terms] [type keywords] [culture keywords] [tier] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.character_objects_any hero troop (returns characters that are EITHER hero OR troop) Example: gm.query.character_objects_any lord wanderer (returns lords OR wanderers) Example: gm.query.character_objects_any cavalry ranged tier4

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.TroopQueryCommands.QueryCharacterObjectsCommand
member.summary: Query ALL CharacterObjects with AND logic. Returns EVERYTHING by default (heroes, troops, templates, NPCs). Use type keywords to filter specific categories. Usage: gm.query.character_objects [search terms] [type keywords] [culture keywords] [tier] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.character_objects imperial hero Example: gm.query.character_objects troop vlandia sort:name Example: gm.query.character_objects lord female empire,vlandia

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.TroopQueryCommands.QueryCharacterObjectsInfoCommand
member.summary: Get detailed info about a specific CharacterObject by ID. Works for ALL character types: heroes, troops, templates, NPCs. Usage: gm.query.character_objects_info [characterId] Example: gm.query.character_objects_info imperial_legionary Example: gm.query.character_objects_info lord_1_1

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.TroopQueryCommands.QueryTroopAnyCommand
member.summary: Find troops matching ANY of the specified types (OR logic) Usage: gm.query.troop_any [search terms] [type keywords] [tier] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.troop_any cavalry ranged (cavalry OR ranged) Example: gm.query.troop_any bow crossbow tier4 Example: gm.query.troop_any empire vlandia infantry

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.TroopQueryCommands.QueryTroopCommand
member.summary: Unified troop listing command with AND logic Usage: gm.query.troop [search terms] [type keywords] [tier] [sort parameters] Example: gm.query.troop imperial infantry Example: gm.query.troop aserai cavalry tier3 Example: gm.query.troop shield infantry sort:tier:desc Example: gm.query.troop battania ranged bow Example: gm.query.troop noble empire tier5

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Console.Query.TroopQueryCommands.QueryTroopInfoCommand
member.summary: Get detailed info about a specific troop by ID Usage: gm.query.troop_info [troopId] Example: gm.query.troop_info imperial_legionary

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureExtensions.ToCultureFlag(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CultureObject)
member.summary: Gets the CultureFlag enum value that corresponds to this culture object.
member.param: The culture object to convert.
member.returns: The corresponding CultureFlag value, or CultureFlags.None if not found or null.

members.member: MARK: Culture Lookup

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureLookup.Nord
member.summary: Returns Nord if War Sails is active, other wise returns Sturgia

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureLookup.Corsairs
member.summary: Returns Corsairs (southern_pirates) if War Sails is active, other wise returns Sea Raiders (sea_raiders)

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureLookup.RandomMainCulture
member.summary: Get a random main culture

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureLookup.RandomBanditCulture(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Get a random bandit culture

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureLookup.RandomHideoutBanditCulture(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns a random bandit culture appropriate for hideouts (excludes looters and optionally deserters).
member.param: If true, includes deserters in the selection pool.
member.returns: A random hideout-appropriate bandit culture.

members.member:
MARK: Hero Name
Gets a unique random hero name for the specified culture and gender. Fallback chain: BLGM custom names -> Native Bannerlord names -> BLGM name + suffix -> Last resort.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureLookup.GetHeroNames(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets hero names for a specific culture and gender

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureLookup.GetHeroSuffixes(System.String)
member.summary: Gets hero name suffixes for a specific culture

members.member:
MARK: Clan Name
Gets a unique random clan name for the specified culture. Fallback chain: BLGM custom names -> Native Bannerlord names -> Prefix + BLGM name -> Last resort.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureLookup.GetClanNames(System.String)
member.summary: Gets clan names for a specific culture

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureLookup.GetFactionPrefixes(System.String)
member.summary: Gets faction name prefixes for a specific culture

members.member:
MARK: Kingdom Name
Gets a unique random kingdom name for the specified culture. Fallback chain: BLGM custom names -> Prefix + BLGM name -> Last resort. Note: No native culture.KingdomNameList exists in Bannerlord, so that step is skipped.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Cultures.CultureLookup.GetKingdomNames(System.String)
member.summary: Gets kingdom names for a specific culture

members.member:
MARK: Culture Flags
Gets the CultureFlag enum value that corresponds to the provided culture object.
The culture object to convert. The corresponding CultureFlag value, or CultureFlags.None if not found or null.

Equips all heroes in the faction with stat-based equipment.
The faction (Clan or Kingdom) whose heroes to equip. Equipment tier (0+). Use -1 for auto-calculation from hero level. Whether to also replace civilian equipment. If true, equips all heroes. If false, only equips BLGM-created heroes. BLGMResult with details of the operation.

MARK: IsBLGMHero

The hero to check. True if the hero was created by BLGM; false otherwise.

MARK: SaveCharacterSetToFile
Saves a hero's full character data to a single JSON file. Aggregates metadata, appearance, development, traits, and both equipment sets.
The hero whose character data is being saved The full file path to save to

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.CharacterSetFileManager.LoadCharacterSetData(System.String)
member.summary: Loads character set data from a JSON file without applying to a hero.
member.param: The full file path to load from
member.returns: The deserialized character set data

members.member:
MARK: LoadCharacterSetToHero
Loads saved character data from a JSON file and applies ALL sections to an existing hero. Applies: age, culture, appearance, development, traits, battle equipment, civilian equipment. Does NOT apply: name, gender, stringId, MBGUID, type (occupation).
The hero to apply data to The full file path to load from BLGMResult with summary of applied/skipped items

MARK: LoadCharacterSetToHero (flags)
Loads saved character data from a JSON file and selectively applies sections to an existing hero. Use to control which sections are applied. Does NOT apply: name, gender, stringId, MBGUID, type (occupation).
The hero to apply data to The full file path to load from Flags controlling which sections to apply BLGMResult with summary of applied/skipped items

MARK: ImportCharacterSet
Creates a brand new hero from saved character data. Uses HeroGenerator to create and initialize the hero, then overrides with saved data.
Full path to the character set JSON file Target clan for the new hero (required) Override hero type from file. If null, uses saved HeroType. Valid: "lord", "wanderer", "companion" Settlement for placement. If null, auto-resolved from clan For Lords only: create a party. Defaults to true BLGMResult with the created hero summary

MARK: ImportFromIndividualSet
Creates a new hero and applies data from a single individual set file (Appearance, Development, Traits, or Equipment). Unlike ImportCharacterSet() which requires a full CharacterSetData, this works with partial set files. Missing metadata (gender, culture, age, level) is resolved from defaults or the clan. The hero is fully initialized with generated skills/traits/equipment, then the individual set overrides its section.
Full path to the individual set JSON file. Which type of individual set file this is. Target clan for the new hero (required). Override hero type: "lord", "wanderer", "companion". Defaults to "lord". Settlement for placement. If null, auto-resolved from clan. For Lords only: create a party. Defaults to true. BLGMResult with the created hero summary.

MARK: CaptureAppearance
Captures a hero's appearance data into an AppearanceSetData object. Mirrors the field-capture logic from AppearanceFileManager.SaveAppearanceToFile().

MARK: CaptureDevelopment
Captures a hero's development data into a DevelopmentSetData object. Mirrors the field-capture logic from DevelopmentFileManager.SaveDevelopmentToFile().

MARK: CaptureTraits
Captures a hero's trait data into a TraitSetData object. Mirrors the field-capture logic from TraitFileManager.SaveTraitsToFile().

MARK: CaptureEquipment
Captures a hero's equipment data into an EquipmentSetData object. Mirrors the field-capture logic from EquipmentFileManager.SaveEquipmentToFile().
The hero whose equipment is being captured True to capture civilian equipment, false for battle

MARK: ApplyAppearance
Applies appearance data to a hero. Mirrors the application logic from AppearanceFileManager.LoadAppearanceToHero(). Gender mismatch is allowed but warned about during character set operations.

MARK: ApplyDevelopment
Applies development data to a hero using the 7-step pattern from DevelopmentFileManager.LoadDevelopmentToHero(). 1. Set Attributes, 2. Set Focus, 3. Set Skill Levels, 4. Clear All Perks, 5. Re-Select Saved Perks, 6. Set Unspent Points, 7. RecalculateLevel.

MARK: ApplyTraits
Applies trait data to a hero. Mirrors the application logic from TraitFileManager.LoadTraitsToHero().

MARK: ApplyEquipment
Applies equipment data to a hero. Mirrors the application logic from EquipmentFileManager.LoadEquipmentFromFile().
The hero to apply equipment to The equipment set data to apply True to apply to civilian equipment, false for battle

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.AttributeEditor
member.summary: Provides unrestricted attribute editing operations for any hero. Uses native HeroDeveloper.AddAttribute (with checkUnspentPoints: false) and RemoveAttribute, both of which are public. No reflection needed.

members.member:
MARK: SetAttribute
Sets a hero's attribute to an exact value. Computes the delta from current and calls AddAttribute or RemoveAttribute accordingly. Bypasses unspent point restrictions by passing checkUnspentPoints: false.
The hero to modify The attribute to set The target attribute value (clamped to 0-MaxAttribute by native) If true, respects the native MaxAttribute cap. If false, bypasses it. BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: AddAttribute
Adds or removes attribute points by a delta amount. Positive delta increases, negative delta decreases. Bypasses unspent point restrictions.
The hero to modify The attribute to change The amount to change (positive to add, negative to remove) BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: GetAttributeInfo
Gets the current value of an attribute for a hero.
The hero to query The attribute to query The current attribute value, or 0 if parameters are null

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.HeroEditorState.#ctor(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Hero,System.Action)
member.summary: Creates a new hero editor state for the specified hero. Parameters must be passed via constructor because the screen is created during CreateState() before any Initialize() method could be called. The HeroSkillEditor is created immediately, capturing the hero's current development state as a snapshot for reset/cancel support.
member.param: The hero whose character development will be edited
Optional callback when editor closes

members.member:
MARK: GetHero
Gets the target hero being edited.

MARK: GetEditor
Gets the HeroSkillEditor instance for modifying the target hero's character development. The editor was created at state construction time with an initial snapshot already captured.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.HeroSkillEditor
member.summary: Primary entry point for unrestricted hero character development editing. Composes SkillEditor, AttributeEditor, FocusEditor, and PerkEditor behind a single facade. Manages snapshot lifecycle for reset/cancel operations. All operations work on any hero and bypass native point restrictions. Both console commands and Commander's UI should call through this class.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.HeroSkillEditor.TargetHero
member.summary: The hero being edited

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.HeroSkillEditor.OriginalSnapshot
member.summary: Snapshot captured at creation time for full reset support

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.HeroSkillEditor.#ctor(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Hero)
member.summary: Creates a new HeroSkillEditor for the specified hero and captures an initial snapshot.
member.param: The hero to edit. Cannot be null.

members.member:
MARK: SetSkillLevel
Sets the target hero's skill to an exact level.

MARK: ChangeSkillLevel
Changes the target hero's skill level by a delta amount.

MARK: AddSkillXp
Injects XP directly into a skill for the target hero.

MARK: GetSkillInfo
Gets detailed skill information for the target hero.

MARK: SetAttribute
Sets the target hero's attribute to an exact value.

MARK: AddAttribute
Adds or removes attribute points for the target hero by a delta amount.

MARK: GetAttributeValue
Gets the current attribute value for the target hero.

MARK: SetFocus
Sets the target hero's focus for a skill to an exact value.

MARK: AddFocus
Adds or removes focus points for the target hero by a delta amount.

MARK: RemoveFocus
Removes focus points from a skill for the target hero.

MARK: GetFocusValue
Gets the current focus value for a skill for the target hero.

MARK: SelectPerk
Selects a perk for the target hero.

MARK: DeselectPerk
Deselects a perk for the target hero. Handles permanent bonus reversal.

MARK: TogglePerk
Toggles a perk on or off for the target hero.

MARK: ClearPerksForSkill
Clears all perks for a specific skill for the target hero.

MARK: ClearAllPerks
Clears all perks for the target hero.

MARK: HasPerk
Checks if the target hero has a specific perk.

MARK: SetUnspentFocusPoints
Directly sets the number of unspent focus points for the target hero.
The number of unspent focus points to set BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: SetUnspentAttributePoints
Directly sets the number of unspent attribute points for the target hero.
The number of unspent attribute points to set BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: ResetAll
Restores the target hero to the state captured when this editor was created.
BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: ResetSkills
Restores only skill levels from the original snapshot.
BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: ResetAttributes
Restores only attribute values from the original snapshot.
BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: ResetFocus
Restores only focus values from the original snapshot.
BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: ResetPerks
Restores only perk selections from the original snapshot. Clears all perks first (with permanent bonus handling), then re-selects original perks.
BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: RecalculateLevel
Recalculates the hero's level from current skill XP totals. Uses the native formula: TotalXp = sum(2 * skillLevel^2.2) - 2000, then checks level from XP. Calls SetInitialLevelFromSkills pattern followed by CheckLevel.
BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: GetSummary
Returns a formatted string containing the current character development state of the target hero. Includes all skills, attributes, focus, unspent points, and level.
Formatted summary string

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.HeroSkillSnapshot
member.summary: Captures the full character development state of a hero at a point in time so it can be restored on cancel/reset. Analogous to how native SkillVM tracks original focus, but captures everything: skills, XP, focus, attributes, perks, unspent points, level, and total XP.

members.member:
MARK: Capture
Captures the full character development state of the specified hero. Iterates Skills.All, Attributes.All, and PerkObject.All to record current values.
The hero whose state to capture A new snapshot containing the hero's current development state, or null if hero is null

MARK: RestoreTo
Restores the captured state back to the specified hero. Uses SetInitialSkillLevel for skills, direct attribute/focus APIs, and perk toggling.
The hero to restore state to BLGMResult indicating success or failure

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.PerkEditor
member.summary: Provides unrestricted perk editing operations for any hero. Uses native HeroDeveloper.AddPerk (public) for selecting perks, and Hero.SetPerkValueInternal (internal, requires reflection) for deselecting. Handles permanent bonus reversal when deselecting perks that grant permanent bonuses.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.PerkEditor.SetPerkValueInternalMethod
member.summary: Cached MethodInfo for Hero.SetPerkValueInternal(PerkObject, bool). This is internal in native code but used by PerkResetCampaignBehavior for perk resets.

members.member:
MARK: SelectPerk
Selects (activates) a perk for a hero. Uses native HeroDeveloper.AddPerk which is public. Does not check skill level requirements.
The hero to modify The perk to select BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: DeselectPerk
Deselects (deactivates) a perk for a hero. Uses reflection to call Hero.SetPerkValueInternal(perk, false) since it is internal. Also handles permanent bonus reversal for perks that grant permanent attribute/focus bonuses.
The hero to modify The perk to deselect BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: TogglePerk
Toggles a perk on or off. If currently selected, deselects it. If not selected, selects it.
The hero to modify The perk to toggle BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: ClearPerksForSkill
Clears all perks for a specific skill. Handles permanent bonus reversal for each perk. Follows the native PerkResetCampaignBehavior.ClearPerksForSkill pattern.
The hero to modify The skill whose perks to clear BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: ClearAllPerks
Clears all perks for a hero. Handles permanent bonus cleanup first, then uses native Hero.ClearPerks() which also adjusts HitPoints.
The hero whose perks to clear BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: HasPerk
Checks if a hero has a specific perk selected.
The hero to check The perk to check for True if the hero has the perk, false otherwise or if parameters are null

MARK: ClearPermanentBonus
Reverses permanent attribute/focus bonuses granted by specific perks. Replicates the logic from native PerkResetCampaignBehavior.ClearPermanentBonusesIfExists(). Only acts if the hero currently has the perk selected.
The hero to remove permanent bonuses from The perk whose permanent bonuses to reverse

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.PerkEditor.InvokeSetPerkValueInternal(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Hero,TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CharacterDevelopment.PerkObject,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Invokes Hero.SetPerkValueInternal via cached reflection.
member.param: The hero instance to invoke on
The perk to set
True to select, false to deselect
member.returns: BLGMResult indicating success or failure

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.SkillEditor
member.summary: Provides unrestricted skill level and XP editing operations for any hero. Uses native HeroDeveloper APIs: SetInitialSkillLevel for bidirectional level changes, AddSkillXp for positive XP injection (bypasses focus factor).

members.member:
MARK: SetSkillLevel
Sets a hero's skill to an exact level by computing and applying the required XP. Works for both increasing and decreasing skill levels. Uses native SetInitialSkillLevel which directly sets XP and skill value.
The hero to modify The skill to set The target skill level (clamped to 0-1023) BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: ChangeSkillLevel
Changes a hero's skill level by a delta amount (positive or negative). For positive deltas, uses native ChangeSkillLevel which adds XP. For negative deltas, uses SetInitialSkillLevel since native AddSkillXp rejects negative XP.
The hero to modify The skill to change The amount to change (positive to increase, negative to decrease) BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: AddSkillXp
Injects XP directly into a skill. Always positive in native API. Bypasses focus factor by passing isAffectedByFocusFactor: false.
The hero to modify The skill to add XP to The amount of XP to add (must be positive) BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: GetSkillInfo
Returns information about a hero's skill including current level, total XP, XP progress toward next level, and XP required for next level.
The hero to query The skill to get info for A SkillInfo struct with skill details, or default if parameters are null

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroDevelopment.SkillInfo
member.summary: Immutable struct containing detailed information about a hero's skill state.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroGenerator
member.summary: Central system for creating heroes with flexible initialization options. Separates hero creation from role initialization to prevent hidden side effects. Now uses only Lord and Wanderer occupation characters to avoid notable occupation conflicts. Uses a continuous algorithm to generate skills for any target level (uncapped).

members.member:
MARK: CreateBasicHero Core
Creates a basic hero from a character object WITHOUT any occupation-specific initialization. This is the layer 1 foundation method - it only creates the hero object with basic properties. Use Initialize methods afterward to set up role-specific state (Lord, Wanderer, Companion). Recommended to use one of the CreateLord, CreateWanderer, etc methods unless you want more control.
IMPORTANT: Source characters should only be Lord or Wanderer occupation to avoid conflicts. CharacterTemplatePooler.GetAllHeroTemplatesFromFlags() ensures this by filtering out notables.
Character to create hero from (Lord or Wanderer occupation only). Hero's name as TextObject. Optional. Hero's age. Minimum age is 18. If not specified (default -1), or if value is less than 18, a random age between 18 and 30 (inclusive) is assigned. Optional. Hero's clan. Can be null for wanderers. Defaults to null. Optional. How much hero appearance is randomized from its base template constraints (0-1). Defaults to 0 (no randomization). Created hero with occupation to be set by Initialize methods.

MARK: InitializeAsLord
Layer 2: Initializes a hero as a Lord with proper occupation, equipment, and optionally creates a party. Hero must have a clan assigned before calling this method.
Hero to initialize as Lord. Must have a clan assigned. Settlement for hero's home (used for party spawn if creating party). Optional. If true, creates a party for the lord if clan is below commander limit. Defaults to true. Optional. Target level for the hero (uncapped). If less than 1 (default -1), a random level between 10 and 25 (inclusive) is assigned. Optional. If true (default), primary skills are combat-oriented. If false, primary skills are management/utility-oriented.

MARK: InitializeAsWanderer
Layer 2: Initializes a hero as a Wanderer (recruitable companion in settlement). Sets clan to null, equips basic gear, and places hero in specified settlement.
Hero to initialize as Wanderer. Settlement where the wanderer will reside. Optional. Target level for the hero (uncapped). If less than 1 (default -1), a random level between 1 and 14 (inclusive) is assigned. Optional. If true (default), primary skills are combat-oriented. If false, primary skills are management/utility-oriented.

MARK: InitializeAsCompanion
Layer 2: Initializes a hero as a Companion ready to be added to a party. Does NOT place hero in settlement - hero is in neutral active state ready for party roster. Use MobilePartyExtensions.AddCompanionToParty() after calling this method.
Hero to initialize as Companion. Optional. Target level for the hero (uncapped). If less than 1 (default -1), a random level between 1 and 14 (inclusive) is assigned. Optional. If true (default), primary skills are combat-oriented. If false, primary skills are management/utility-oriented.

MARK: CleanupHeroState
Cleans up a hero's state by removing them from parties and settlements. Useful when moving heroes between roles or clans.
Hero to clean up.

MARK: CreateLord
Creates a lord with the specified name and culture, optionally with a party. This is a high-level Layer 3 convenience method that combines creation (Layer 1) and initialization (Layer 2). Random level range for lords: 10-25.
Name for the lord. Culture pool to select character template from. Gender selection for character template. Clan for the lord (required). Optional. If true, creates a party for the lord if clan is below commander limit. Defaults to true. Optional. Home settlement for the lord. Defaults to null (auto-resolved). Optional. Appearance randomization factor (0-1). Defaults to 0.5. Optional. Target level for the hero (uncapped). If less than 1 (default -1), a random level between 10 and 25 (inclusive) is assigned. Optional. Hero's age. Minimum age is 18. If not specified (default -1), or if value is less than 18, a random age between 18 and 30 (inclusive) is assigned. Optional. If true (default), primary skills are combat-oriented. If false, primary skills are management/utility-oriented. Created and initialized lord hero.

MARK: CreateLords
Creates multiple lords with random names from culture. This is a high-level Layer 3 convenience method that combines creation (Layer 1) and initialization (Layer 2). Random level range for lords: 10-25.
Number of lords to create. Culture pool to select character templates from. Gender selection for character templates. Clan for the lords (required). Optional. If true, creates a party for each lord if clan is below commander limit. Defaults to true. Optional. Home settlement for the lords. Defaults to null (auto-resolved). Optional. Appearance randomization factor (0-1). Defaults to 0.5. Optional. Target level for each hero (uncapped). If less than 1 (default -1), a random level between 10 and 25 (inclusive) is assigned per hero. Optional. Age for each hero. Minimum age is 18. If not specified (default -1), or if value is less than 18, a random age between 18 and 30 (inclusive) is assigned per hero. Optional. If true (default), primary skills are combat-oriented. If false, primary skills are management/utility-oriented. List of created and initialized lord heroes.

MARK: CreateWanderer
Creates a wanderer (recruitable companion) at the specified settlement. Layer 3: Convenience method, automatically performing Layer 1 and Layer 2 operations. Random level range for wanderers: 1-14.
Name for the wanderer. Culture pool to select character template from. Gender selection for character template. Settlement where the wanderer will reside. Optional. Appearance randomization factor (0-1). Defaults to 0.5. Optional. Target level for the hero (uncapped). If less than 1 (default -1), a random level between 1 and 14 (inclusive) is assigned. Optional. Hero's age. Minimum age is 18. If not specified (default -1), or if value is less than 18, a random age between 18 and 30 (inclusive) is assigned. Optional. If true (default), primary skills are combat-oriented. If false, primary skills are management/utility-oriented. Created and initialized wanderer hero.

MARK: CreateWanderers
Creates multiple wanderers with random names at the specified settlement. Layer 3: Convenience method, automatically performing Layer 1 and Layer 2 operations. Random level range for wanderers: 1-14.
Number of wanderers to create. Culture pool to select character templates from. Gender selection for character templates. Settlement where the wanderers will reside. Optional. Appearance randomization factor (0-1). Defaults to 0.5. Optional. Target level for each hero (uncapped). If less than 1 (default -1), a random level between 1 and 14 (inclusive) is assigned per hero. Optional. Age for each hero. Minimum age is 18. If not specified (default -1), or if value is less than 18, a random age between 18 and 30 (inclusive) is assigned per hero. Optional. If true (default), primary skills are combat-oriented. If false, primary skills are management/utility-oriented. List of created and initialized wanderer heroes.

MARK: CreateCompanions
Creates heroes ready to be added as party companions (no settlement state). Layer 3: Convenience method, automatically performing Layer 1 and Layer 2 operations. Use MobilePartyExtensions.AddCompanionsToParty() after calling this method. Random level range for companions: 1-14.
Number of companions to create. Culture pool to select character templates from. Optional. Gender selection for character templates. Defaults to GenderFlags.Either. Optional. Appearance randomization factor (0-1). Defaults to 0.5. Optional. Target level for each hero (uncapped). If less than 1 (default -1), a random level between 1 and 14 (inclusive) is assigned per hero. Optional. Age for each hero. Minimum age is 18. If not specified (default -1), or if value is less than 18, a random age between 18 and 30 (inclusive) is assigned per hero. Optional. If true (default), primary skills are combat-oriented. If false, primary skills are management/utility-oriented. List of created and initialized companion heroes ready for party assignment.

MARK: InitializeTraits
Set random Valor, Mercy, Honor, Calculating, and Commander traits
The hero to set the traits for Optional, defaults to true. If true gives commander trait

MARK: CreateHeroFromMetadata
Creates a fully initialized hero from explicit metadata parameters. This is the reusable building block for both full character set imports and individual set imports. Handles: template selection, CreateBasicHero, and InitializeAs* based on heroType.
Hero name. Required. Target clan. Required for lords and companions. Role type: "lord", "wanderer", "companion". Defaults to "lord". Culture StringId for template selection. If null, uses clan's culture. Gender for template selection. If null, random (Either). Hero age. If less than 18, random 18-30. Target level. If less than 1, type-appropriate random. Placement settlement. If null, auto-resolved from clan. For lords: create party. Defaults to true. The created and initialized hero, or null on failure (logged via BLGMResult).
