members.member:
MARK: RegenerateSkillsForLevel
Public entry point to regenerate a hero's skills, attributes, focus, and perks for a target level. Clears existing character development state and rebuilds from scratch using the skill distribution algorithm. Useful for reviving dead heroes whose OnDeath() cleared their skills/perks/traits. If targetLevel is less than 1, uses the hero's current Level value as the target.
The hero whose skills will be regenerated. HeroDeveloper must not be null. Target level. If less than 1, defaults to hero.Level (minimum 1). If true, combat skills are primary. If false, noncombat skills are primary.

MARK: InitializeSkillsForLevel
Initializes hero skills and attributes to match a target level using a continuous algorithm. This clears existing skills, computes an XP budget from the native level formula, distributes skill values across combat/noncombat/utility categories, and validates the result lands on the exact target level. Supports any level (uncapped).
The hero whose skills will be initialized. The desired level (minimum 1, uncapped). If true, combat skills are primary. If false, noncombat skills are primary.

MARK: BuildSkillCategories
Builds the three-tier skill category lists based on combat/noncombat focus. Primary skills get the highest values, secondary gets medium, tertiary gets lowest. Skills within each pool are shuffled so specializations vary per hero.
If true, combat pool is primary and noncombat is tertiary. If false, reversed. Output list for primary (highest) skills. Output list for secondary (medium) skills. Output list for tertiary (lowest) skills.

MARK: ComputeBaseSkillValue
Computes the base skill value for a given tier using the inverse of the native XP formula. Distributes the total XP budget across all skills using weighted multipliers, then converts each tier's XP share back to a skill value: skillValue = (xpShare / 2)^(1/2.2).
The total raw XP sum (before the -2000 offset) to distribute. Number of primary tier skills. Number of secondary tier skills. Number of tertiary tier skills. The weight multiplier for the tier being computed. The base skill value for the specified tier.

MARK: ApplySkillsWithVariance
Applies skill values to a hero with random variance for natural-feeling distributions. Each skill gets the base value +/- SkillVariancePercent randomized.
The hero to assign skills to. List of skills in this tier. The base skill value for this tier.

MARK: ValidateAndAdjustSkills
Validates that the hero's current skill distribution produces the correct target level. If the total XP is too high (would overshoot to next level), scales down the highest skill. If too low (would undershoot), scales up the highest skill. Uses iterative correction with a maximum number of passes.
The hero whose skills to validate. The desired level. The minimum XP required for the target level. The XP threshold for the next level (must stay below this).

MARK: ComputeCurrentXpSum
Computes the raw XP sum from all current skill values using the native formula. Raw sum = Sum(2 * skillValue^2.2) across all 18 skills. TotalXp is then rawSum - 2000.
The hero to compute XP sum for. The raw XP sum before the -2000 offset.

MARK: SkillValueFromXpContribution
Converts an XP contribution amount back to a skill value using the inverse formula. Given xpContrib = 2 * skill^2.2, solves for: skill = (xpContrib / 2)^(1/2.2).
The XP contribution to convert (can be the contribution of a single skill). The skill value that would produce the given XP contribution.

MARK: ComputeSkillsRequiredForLevel
Replicates the native SkillsRequiredForLevel formula without the level 62 cap. Uses long arithmetic to avoid overflow at high levels. Native formula: starts at gap=1000, each level gap += 1000 + gap/5.
Target level to compute XP threshold for. The cumulative TotalXp required to reach the specified level.

MARK: SelectRandomTemplate
Selects a random character template from the given culture/gender pool. Only returns Lord and Wanderer occupation characters (no notables). Creates a new CharacterTemplatePooler internally.
Culture pool to select from. Gender selection filter. A copy of a randomly selected CharacterObject template.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroGenerator.SelectRandomTemplate(System.Collections.Generic.List{TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CharacterObject})
member.summary: Selects a random character from the given pool and creates a copy of it.
member.param: Pre-built pool of character templates to select from.
member.returns: A copy of a randomly selected CharacterObject from the pool.

members.member:
MARK: ShuffleList
Fisher-Yates shuffle for randomizing skill selection order.
Type of elements in the list. List to shuffle in-place.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroManager.GetBestInitialSettlement(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Hero)
member.summary: tries to get a random settlement in this order: From heroes clan > from heroes kingdom > from all settlements

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroManager.TrySetHomeSettlement(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Hero,TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement)
member.summary: Uses reflection to try to the Heroes home settlement directly
member.returns: BLGM result containing bool if Setting homeSettlement succeeded and a string with details

members.member:
MARK: ActivateHero
Activates a non-Active hero, placing them at a settlement. Handles all hero states: Prisoner, Fugitive, Released, Disabled, NotSpawned, Traveling, and Dead. Dead heroes are revived -- if OnDeath() was called, skills/equipment are reconstructed. Heroes already Active are returned with a success message (no-op).
The hero to activate Optional settlement to place the hero. If null, auto-resolved via GetHomeOrAlternativeSettlement() BLGMResult with success/failure details

MARK: ReviveHero
Revives a dead hero by restoring their state, fixing death day, and reconstructing nulled fields if OnDeath() was called. This is a private helper for ActivateHero().

MARK: ReconstructDeadHeroFields
Reconstructs fields that were nulled by Hero.OnDeath(). Uses reflection to restore private fields to default-constructed state, regenerates skills based on the hero's preserved Level, then re-equips with appropriate gear.

MARK: Impregnate
Makes a female hero pregnant with an optional specified father. If no father is specified, resolves one automatically via ResolveFather(). Uses reflection to replace the pregnancy record with the correct father after MakePregnantAction.Apply().
The female hero to make pregnant Optional father hero. If null, will be auto-resolved. BLGMResult with success/failure details

MARK: GiveBirth (no father)
Forces immediate birth when the father is not known/provided. Resolves father from the pregnancy record, or falls back to PregnancyHelpers.ResolveFather().
The pregnant hero to deliver True for a female child, false for male BLGMResult with success/failure details

MARK: GiveBirth
Forces immediate birth with an explicitly provided father. Delivers the offspring, fires native birth events, clears pregnancy state, and removes the pregnancy record.
The pregnant hero to deliver The father hero True for a female child, false for male BLGMResult with success/failure details

MARK: AbortBirth
Terminates a pregnancy without delivering a child. Removes the pregnancy record first, then clears the pregnancy flag to maintain consistent state.
The pregnant hero whose pregnancy to abort BLGMResult with success/failure details

MARK: Marry
Marry two heroes. Divorces both from current spouses first if needed. Tries native MarriageAction.Apply() first, only forces via Spouse setter when forceMarriage is true.
First hero (otherHero joins this hero's clan by default) Second hero If true, bypasses native validation checks on failure If true, otherHero joins hero's clan. If false, both heroes stay in original clans BLGMResult with success/failure details

MARK: Divorce
Divorce hero from their current spouse. The native Spouse setter handles both sides and exSpouses lists.
The hero to divorce from their current spouse BLGMResult with success/failure details

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroOutfitter
member.summary: Provides methods for equipping heroes intelligently with appropriate gear.

members.member:
MARK: EquipHeroByStats
Equips a hero with stat-based equipment using the new equipment generation system. Analyzes hero skills to determine optimal weapon loadout. This is the recommended method for equipping heroes.
The hero to equip. Equipment tier (0-6). Use -1 for auto-calculation from hero level. Whether to also replace civilian equipment. BLGMResult indicating success or failure.

MARK: EquipHeroWithOption
Equips a hero with equipment using explicit configuration options. Use this when you need full control over the equipment generation.
The hero to equip. Culture for item selection (null = use hero's culture). Equipment tier (0-6). Explicit weapon type preferences. Whether to include horse equipment. Whether to replace battle equipment. Whether to replace civilian equipment. BLGMResult indicating success or failure.

MARK: GenerateEquipment
Generates equipment for a hero without applying it. Useful for preview or manual modification before assignment.
The hero to generate equipment for. Equipment tier (0-6). Use -1 for auto-calculation. Whether to generate civilian equipment. Generated Equipment object, or null if hero is null.

MARK: GetTierForHero
Gets the appropriate equipment tier for a hero based on their level.
The hero to evaluate. Equipment tier (0-6).

MARK: WeaponPreferences
Derives weapon type preferences from a hero's combat skills.
The hero to analyze. WeaponTypeFlags representing preferred weapon types.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.HeroPositionHelpers
member.summary: Provides helpers for finding heroes based on position, proximity, and settlement location.

members.member:
MARK: GetHeroMapPosition
Gets the best available map position for a hero. Checks settlement first, then party, then party's attached-to party.
The hero to get the position for The resolved map position True if a valid position was found, false otherwise

MARK: GetHeroesInSettlement
Collects all heroes present in a settlement, including heroes without a party and hero members of parties stationed at the settlement.
The settlement to search Output list of heroes found in the settlement

MARK: FindNearestHeroes
Finds the nearest heroes to a given map position from Hero.AllAliveHeroes, filtered by a predicate. Uses DistanceSquared for performance. Returns up to maxCount heroes sorted by distance (closest first).
The map position to measure distance from Maximum number of heroes to return Filter predicate (return true to include the hero) List of up to maxCount nearest heroes matching the predicate

Finds a hero with the specified heroId, Fast but case sensitive (all string ids SHOULD be lower case)
Use QueryHeroes().FirstOrDefault() to find hero with case insensitive partial name or partial stringIds

Performance focused method to find heroes matching multiple parameters. All parameters are optional and can be used with none, one or a combination of any parameters
Note: parameter is a string used that will match partial hero names or partial stringIds

Hero type flags that ALL must match (AND logic)
If true, hero must have ALL flags. If false, hero must have ANY flag
If true, searches dead heroes instead of alive ones
Sort field (id, name, age, clan, kingdom, or any HeroType flag)
True for descending, false for ascending

member.summary: Identifies the type of an individual set file for import operations. Used by CharacterSetFileManager.ImportFromIndividualSet() to determine which file manager to use for loading and which section to apply.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.PregnancyHelpers
member.summary: Helper methods for pregnancy father resolution and candidate searching.

members.member:
MARK: ValidatePregnancy
Validates if two heroes are able to conceive a child with each other. Does not resolve father -- caller must provide both heroes. Will log any errors or failed validations
BLGMResult with success or specific error details

MARK: ResolveFather
Resolves the father for a pregnancy in priority order:
1. Explicit father if provided
2. Mother's spouse if male
3. Random male hero in mother's current settlement
4. Random from 10 closest male heroes on the map
The mother hero Explicitly specified father, or null for auto-resolution The resolved father hero, or null if no valid candidate found

MARK: FindFatherInSettlement
Searches the mother's current settlement for a valid male hero to be the father. Includes heroes with and without parties.

MARK: FindFatherNearby
Searches for the 10 closest male heroes on the map and picks one randomly.

MARK: IsFatherCandidate
Checks if a hero is a valid father candidate for the given mother. Must be male, alive, not the player hero, and not in the mother's clan.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Heroes.PregnancyReflectionHelper
member.summary: Provides cached reflection access to PregnancyCampaignBehavior internals for manipulating pregnancy records (replacing father on existing pregnancies).

members.member:
MARK: ReplacePregnancyFather
Replaces the father on an existing pregnancy record for the given mother. Must be called after MakePregnantAction.Apply() has created the initial record.
The pregnant hero whose pregnancy record to modify The hero to set as the father BLGMResult indicating success or failure with details

MARK: GetPregnancyFather
Reads the father from an existing pregnancy record for a given mother.
The pregnant hero whose pregnancy father to retrieve The father Hero, or null if no record found or reflection fails

MARK: RemovePregnancyRecord
Removes the pregnancy record for a given mother from the _heroPregnancies list.
The hero whose pregnancy record to remove BLGMResult indicating success or failure with details

MARK: AddPregnancyRecord
Creates a new pregnancy record and adds it to _heroPregnancies via reflection. Used for clanless mothers where MakePregnantAction.Apply() is bypassed to avoid NRE in PregnancyLogEntry.IsVisibleNotification (mother.Clan.Equals() crashes when Clan is null).
The hero to create a pregnancy record for The hero to set as the father BLGMResult indicating success or failure with details

members.member:
MARK: SelectBannerForHero
Selects an appropriate banner for a hero based on their level and combat skills. Only heroes level 10+ receive banners.
The hero to select a banner for. The hero's level (used for tier calculation). The hero's current equipment (used to check for shield, mount, weapons). An appropriate banner ItemObject, or null if hero is under level 10.

MARK: GetTierIndexFromLevel
Gets the tier index (0, 1, or 2) based on hero level. Level 10-14: Index 0 (Tier 2 items with t1 suffix) Level 15-19: Index 1 (Tier 4 items with t2 suffix) Level 20+: Index 2 (Tier 6 items with t3 suffix)

MARK: IsBannerValidForHero
Validates that a banner is appropriate for a hero based on their equipment. Prevents equipping charge banners to non-mounted, range banners to non-ranged, etc.

MARK: GetBannerByStringId
Gets a banner ItemObject by its string ID.

MARK: GetFallbackBanner
Gets a safe fallback banner that respects hero equipment restrictions.

MARK: HasShieldEquipped
Checks if the equipment has a shield in any weapon slot.

MARK: HasPolearmEquipped
Checks if the equipment has a polearm in any weapon slot.

MARK: HasBowOrCrossbowEquipped
Checks if the equipment has a bow or crossbow in any weapon slot.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.CivilianItemPoolManager
member.summary: Singleton manager that extracts civilian equipment items directly from game equipment rosters based on their EquipmentFlags. This provides more authentic and culture-appropriate civilian outfits compared to heuristic-based item selection.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.CivilianItemPoolManager.Instance
member.summary: Gets the singleton instance of the CivilianItemPoolManager.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.CivilianItemPoolManager.IsInitialized
member.summary: Gets whether the civilian pools have been initialized.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.CivilianItemPoolManager.RostersProcessed
member.summary: Gets the total number of rosters processed during initialization.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.CivilianItemPoolManager.ItemsExtracted
member.summary: Gets the number of items extracted to pools.

members.member:
MARK: Initialize
Initializes or reinitializes civilian item pools by scanning equipment rosters. Call this after game data is fully loaded.

MARK: Clear
Clears all civilian pools and resets initialization state.

MARK: Reinitialize
Reinitializes the civilian pools by clearing and rebuilding them.
MARK: InitializePoolStructures

MARK: ProcessRoster
Processes a single equipment roster and extracts civilian items.

MARK: IsPeasantRoster
Checks if the roster is a peasant roster that should be excluded. These rosters have the noble flag but contain peasant items.

MARK: ResolveCultureId
Resolves the culture ID for a roster. For lord_ rosters with null culture, maps them to the correct culture based on the numeric prefix.

MARK: ExtractCivilianItems
Extracts civilian equipment items from a roster into the appropriate pools.

MARK: ExtractArmorFromEquipment
Extracts armor items from a single equipment set. Filters items by gender suitability to prevent female-only items (e.g., "Ladies Shoes") from being added to male pools.

MARK: ExtractWeaponsFromEquipment
Extracts one-handed civilian weapons from equipment for male characters.

MARK: GetRandomItem
Gets a random item from the appropriate civilian pool for the specified slot. Applies appearance filtering based on hero status (ruling clan members get higher appearance items). Falls back to other cultures if the specific culture has no items.
The culture ID to select from. Whether the hero is female. The equipment slot. Whether the hero is a member of a ruling clan. Additional appearance requirement (0 or 1) for higher quality items. A random item from the pool that meets appearance requirements, or null if none available.

MARK: GetRandomNonCrownHeadItem
Gets a random head item that is NOT a crown. Used for non-ruling clan members who should not wear crowns.
The culture ID to select from. Whether the hero is female. Whether the hero is a member of a ruling clan. A random non-crown head item meeting appearance requirements, or null if none available.

MARK: GetCrown
Gets a crown for ruling clan members.

MARK: GetCivilianWeapon
Gets a one-handed civilian weapon for male characters.

MARK: HasItemsForCulture
Checks if there are civilian items available for a specific culture and gender.
MARK: EnsureInitialized
MARK: EnsureCulturePoolsExist
MARK: EnsureSlotPoolExists

MARK: SelectRandomItem
Selects a random item from a list using the shared random number generator.

MARK: FilterByAppearance
Filters items by civilian appearance requirements.
The list of items to filter. Whether the hero is a member of a ruling clan. A new list containing only items meeting appearance requirements.

MARK: FilterByAppearanceWithBonus
Filters items by civilian appearance requirements with an optional appearance bonus. Used to select higher quality items (20% chance per slot in civilian equipment).
The list of items to filter. Whether the hero is a member of a ruling clan. Additional appearance requirement (0 or 1). A new list containing only items meeting the boosted appearance requirements.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder
member.summary: Builds equipment sets for heroes by selecting items from categorized pools based on culture, tier, stats, and weapon preferences.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.MaleBattleCapeEquipChance
member.summary: Chance for males to equip cape in battle gear (80%).

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.FemaleBattleCapeEquipChance
member.summary: Chance for females to equip cape in battle gear (50%).

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the EquipmentBuilder class.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.#ctor(Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the EquipmentBuilder class with a specific pool manager.
member.param: The item pool manager to use for equipment selection.

members.member:
MARK: WeaponLoadout
Represents a weapon loadout plan for equipment generation. Determines what types of weapons go in which slots based on allowed flags.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.WeaponLoadout.PrimaryMeleeType
member.summary: Primary melee weapon type flags (one-handed, two-handed, or polearm).

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.WeaponLoadout.RangedType
member.summary: Ranged weapon type (Bow, Crossbow, or None). Mutually exclusive.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.WeaponLoadout.ThrowingType
member.summary: Throwing weapon type flags if throwing is allowed.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.WeaponLoadout.IncludeShield
member.summary: Whether a shield should be included.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.WeaponLoadout.IsTwoHanded
member.summary: Whether the primary weapon is two-handed (prevents shield).

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.WeaponLoadout.RangedIsPrimary
member.summary: Whether ranged is the primary skill (goes in slot 0).

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.WeaponLoadout.ThrowingSlots
member.summary: Number of throwing weapon slots needed (0-2).

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.WeaponLoadout.AmmoSlots
member.summary: Number of ammo slots needed for ranged weapons (1-2).

members.member:
MARK: GetEquipmentSet
Generates a complete equipment set based on the specified parameters.
The culture to use for item selection. If null, uses neutral items. The hero's level used to determine tier range. Flags indicating preferred weapon types. Whether to include horse and harness equipment. Whether to include a banner in slot 4. Whether the hero is female (affects armor filtering). Whether to also check neutral item pools. A new Equipment object populated with selected items.

MARK: GetEquipmentSet (Hero overload)
Generates a complete equipment set based on the specified parameters. Uses the hero's skills to determine ranged vs throwing priority when both are specified. For heroes level 10+, selects skill-appropriate banners when withBanner is true.
The hero to use for skill-based priority determination. The culture to use for item selection. If null, uses neutral items. The hero's level used to determine tier range. Flags indicating preferred weapon types. Whether to include horse and harness equipment. Whether to include a banner in slot 4 (only for level 10+). Whether the hero is female (affects armor filtering). Whether to also check neutral item pools. A new Equipment object populated with selected items.

MARK: GetEquipmentForHero
Generates a complete equipment set for a hero based on their stats and culture.
The hero to generate equipment for. The target tier for equipment (0-6). If -1, tier is calculated from hero level. Flags indicating preferred weapon types. If None, preferences are derived from hero skills. Whether to generate civilian equipment (no weapons/armor). A new Equipment object populated with selected items.

MARK: DetermineRangedPriority
Determines whether ranged weapons (bow/crossbow) should take priority over throwing weapons based on the hero's skill levels. When both ranged and throwing are specified in weapon preferences, compares the hero's bow/crossbow skill against throwing skill.
The hero whose skills determine priority. If null, returns true (ranged priority). The weapon type flags to check for ranged/throwing conflict. True if ranged skill >= throwing skill (tie goes to ranged), or if hero is null.

MARK: DetermineWeaponSet
Analyzes weapon type flags to determine the weapon loadout plan.
The allowed weapon type flags. When both ranged and throwing are specified, if true ranged is primary; if false throwing is primary. A WeaponLoadout structure containing the loadout plan.

MARK: FillWeaponSlots
Fills weapon slots 0-3 based on the loadout plan. Slot 0: Primary weapon (NEVER throwing/shields/ammo) Slots 1-3: Secondary weapons, ammo, shields, throwing Each weapon slot gets independent tier expansion roll (70% no change, 20% +/-1, 10% +/-2).
Key loadout rules enforced: - Polearms ALWAYS require a sidearm (sword/axe/mace) - Bows/Crossbows ALWAYS require matching ammo (only if weapon exists) - Multiple throwing weapons must be the same type - Bow + Polearm: bow, 1 arrow, polearm, sidearm - Bow + No Polearm: bow, 2 arrows, sidearm

MARK: FillMeleeOnlyLoadout
Fills weapon slots with melee-only loadout. Enforces sidearm requirement when polearm is primary. Uses specialized polearm selection to ensure mounted heroes get couchable polearms and infantry heroes get braceable polearms. Each weapon slot gets independent tier expansion roll (70% no change, 20% +/-1, 10% +/-2).

MARK: SelectSidearmWeapon
Selects a sidearm weapon (one-handed sword, axe, or mace). Used when polearm is equipped to ensure hero has a backup melee weapon. Implements tier fallback to guarantee a sidearm is found.

MARK: SelectPolearmForMounted
Selects a polearm for mounted heroes with proper priority: 1. Couchable polearms in tier range (preferred for mounted) 2. Any mounted-usable polearm in tier range (non-braceable) 3. Tier fallback only if NO mounted-usable polearm found NEVER returns a braceable-only polearm for mounted heroes.

MARK: CollectMountedPolearmsFromTierRange
Collects mounted-usable polearms from a tier range, separating couchable from other types.

MARK: CollectMountedPolearmsFromPool
Collects mounted-usable polearms from a weapon pool, categorizing by couchable vs other.

MARK: SelectPolearmForMountedWithTierFallback
Tier fallback for mounted polearm selection - expands search to other tiers.

MARK: SelectPolearmForInfantry
Selects a polearm for infantry heroes with proper priority: 1. Braceable polearms in tier range (best for infantry anti-cavalry) 2. Any polearm in tier range (couchable is fine on foot too) 3. Tier fallback only if NO polearm found at all

MARK: CollectInfantryPolearmsFromTierRange
Collects polearms for infantry from a tier range, separating braceable from other types.

MARK: CollectInfantryPolearmsFromPool
Collects polearms for infantry from a weapon pool, categorizing by braceable vs other.

MARK: CalculateExpandedTierRange
Calculates expanded tier range for a single equipment slot. Performs one roll to determine tier expansion: - 70% chance: no change - 20% chance: expand max tier by +1, expand min tier by -1 - 10% chance: expand max tier by +2, expand min tier by -2
Max tier of 6 is treated as unlimited (no cap). Min tier cannot go below 0 (Tier0).
The base minimum tier from level calculation. The base maximum tier from level calculation. Tuple of (expandedMinTier, expandedMaxTier).

MARK: FillArmorSlots
Fills armor slots 5-9 (Head, Cape, Body, Gloves, Boots). For the Head slot, applies crown restrictions based on ruling clan membership. Cape slot has gender-specific empty chance (80% male, 50% female). Each slot gets independent tier expansion roll (70% no change, 20% +/-1, 10% +/-2).
The equipment to fill. The culture to use for item selection. The hero's level used for tier calculations. The minimum item tier. The maximum item tier. Whether the hero is female. Whether to include neutral items. The hero to check for ruling clan membership (can be null).

MARK: FillHorseSlots
Fills horse slots 10-11 (Horse, HorseHarness). Horse is ALWAYS equipped if requested, harness MUST be found with tier fallback.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder.BannerSlotIndex
member.summary: Banner slot index (slot 4 in equipment).

members.member:
MARK: FillBannerSlot
Fills banner slot 4 with a culture-appropriate banner.

MARK: FillBannerSlotForHero
Fills banner slot 4 with a skill-appropriate banner for the hero. Uses BannerSelector to determine the best banner based on hero's level and combat skills. Only called for heroes level 10+.
The equipment to fill (should already have weapons/armor/horse populated). The hero to select a banner for. The hero's level for tier calculation.

MARK: SelectRandomItem
Selects a random item from the given list. Skips items that are not ready or are DefaultItems.Trash. Retries up to the list size to find a valid item.
The list of items to select from. A random item from the list, or null if no valid items found.

MARK: SelectWeightedRandomItem
[Deprecated] This method is unused - all selection methods use SelectRandomItem() with uniform probability. Kept for potential future use. Selects a random item with weighted probability favoring higher tiers. Skips items that are not ready or are DefaultItems.Trash. Weight formula: tier + 2 (so Tier1=3, Tier2=4, ... Tier6=8)
The list of items to select from. A random item from the list (favoring higher tiers), or null if empty.

MARK: GetItemWeight
[Deprecated] Used only by SelectWeightedRandomItem() which is itself deprecated/unused. Gets the selection weight for an item based on its tier. Higher tiers have higher weights for increased selection probability.

MARK: SelectWeaponByType
Selects a weapon matching the specified type from pools. Uses UNIFORM selection from all items within the tier range.

MARK: CollectMatchWeapons
Collects weapons matching the specified type flags into the candidates list. Uses IsPolearmUsableOnMount() for polearms to properly exclude braceable-only polearms from mounted heroes.

MARK: SelectArmorBySlot
Selects armor for a specific slot matching culture and tier criteria. Uses UNIFORM selection from all items within the tier range. Implements tier fallback logic: if no items found in requested tier range, tries lower tiers first, then higher tiers to ensure slot gets filled. Also applies appearance filtering with fallback to no appearance filter.

MARK: CollectArmorFromTierRange
Helper method to collect armor from a specific tier range into candidates list. When includeNeutralItems is true, also includes Calradian (generic) items and Culture=null items.
The culture ID for item selection. The minimum item tier. The maximum item tier. The equipment slot being filled. Whether the hero is female. Whether to exclude cloth armor. Whether to include neutral items. The output list to add valid items to. Whether to apply battle armor appearance filter.

MARK: SelectBattleHeadArmor
Selects head armor for battle equipment with crown restrictions and appearance filtering. Only ruling clan members can wear crowns in battle, and battle crowns must have "battle" or "helmet" in name (or be Jeweled Crown which works in both contexts). Applies appearance filtering with fallback to no appearance filter.
The culture ID for item selection. The minimum item tier. The maximum item tier. The hero's level used for cloth armor exclusion. Whether the hero is female. Whether the hero is a member of the ruling clan. Whether to include neutral items. A suitable head armor item, or null if none found.

MARK: CollectBattleHeadArmorFromTier
Helper method to collect battle head armor from a specific tier.

MARK: CollectBattleHeadArmor
Collects valid head armor items for battle equipment applying gender, cloth, appearance, and crown filters. Crown handling: - Non-ruling clan members cannot wear crowns - Ruling clan members can only wear battle crowns (with "battle" or "helmet" in name) or Jeweled Crown - Jeweled Crown is female-only
The source list of head armor items. Whether the hero is female. Whether to exclude cloth armor. Whether the hero is a member of the ruling clan. The output list to add valid items to. Whether to apply battle armor appearance filter.

MARK: CollectValidArmor
Collects valid armor items applying gender, cloth, and appearance filters.
The source list of armor items. Whether the hero is female. Whether to exclude cloth armor. The output list to add valid items to. Whether to apply battle armor appearance filter.

MARK: SelectShield
Selects a shield matching culture and tier criteria.

MARK: SelectAmmo
Selects ammunition of the specified type.

MARK: SelectHorse
Selects a horse matching culture and tier criteria.

MARK: SelectHarness
Selects a horse harness matching culture and tier criteria.

MARK: SelectHorseWithFallback
Selects a horse with tier fallback to guarantee a horse is found.

MARK: SelectHarnessWithFallback
Selects a horse harness with tier fallback to GUARANTEE a harness is found. Horses ALWAYS require harness/armor/saddle.

MARK: SelectBanner
Selects a banner for the specified culture.

MARK: GetCivilianEquipmentSet
Generates a complete civilian equipment set for a hero using roster-based item pools. Items are extracted directly from game equipment rosters based on their EquipmentFlags, providing more authentic and culture-appropriate civilian outfits. Each armor slot has a 20% chance to get +1 appearance bonus for higher quality items.
Slot selection rules: - Body: 100% (always equip) - Head: Ruling clan = 100% crown; Others = 20% chance, no crowns allowed - Cape: 50% chance - Gloves: Females 10%, Males 100% - Legs: 100% (always equip) - Weapon0: Males only (one civilian melee weapon, no appearance bonus)
The hero to generate equipment for (used for crown eligibility). The culture to use for item selection. If null, uses fallback items. The hero's level (unused in roster-based selection but kept for API compatibility). Whether the hero is female (affects equipment selection and slot chances). Unused in roster-based selection but kept for API compatibility. A new Equipment object with civilian equipment.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.HeroEquipper
member.summary: Provides methods for equipping heroes with dynamically generated equipment based on their stats, culture, and weapon preferences. Analyzes hero combat skills to determine appropriate weapon loadouts.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.HeroEquipper.MinimumSkillThreshold
member.summary: Minimum skill threshold for a skill to be considered significant. Skills below this value are ignored when determining weapon preferences.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.HeroEquipper.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the HeroEquipper class.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.HeroEquipper.#ctor(Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.EquipmentBuilder)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the HeroEquipper class with a specific equipment builder.
member.param: The equipment builder to use for generating equipment.

members.member:
MARK: EquipHeroByStats
Equips a hero with equipment generated based on their stats and culture. This method analyzes the hero's skills to determine optimal weapon preferences and selects appropriate tier equipment.
The hero to equip. The target tier for equipment (0+). Native items are tier 0-6, mods may add higher. If -1, tier is calculated from hero level. Flags indicating preferred weapon types. If None, preferences are derived from hero skills. Whether to replace the hero's battle equipment. Whether to replace the hero's civilian equipment. BLGMResult indicating success or failure with details.

MARK: EquipHero
Equips a hero with equipment generated based on specified parameters.
The hero to equip. The culture to use for item selection. If null, uses hero's culture. The target tier for equipment (0+). Native items are tier 0-6, mods may add higher. Flags indicating preferred weapon types. Whether to include horse and harness equipment. Whether to replace the hero's battle equipment. Whether to replace the hero's civilian equipment. BLGMResult indicating success or failure with details.

MARK: GenerateEquipment
Generates equipment for a hero without applying it. Useful for previewing or modifying equipment before assignment.
The hero to generate equipment for. The target tier for equipment (0+). Native items are tier 0-6, mods may add higher. If -1, tier is calculated from hero level. Flags indicating preferred weapon types. If None, preferences are derived from hero skills. Whether to generate civilian equipment. A new Equipment object populated with selected items, or null if hero is null.

MARK: CalcTierFromLevel
Calculates the appropriate equipment tier based on hero level. Returns tier 6 for high-level heroes (level 31+). For higher tiers from mods, specify the tier explicitly when calling equip methods.
The hero's level. An equipment tier value (0-6 for native levels, higher levels return 6 as base).

MARK: CalcLevelFromTier
Calculates a representative hero level for a given tier. Used when tier is explicitly specified to get appropriate equipment.
The equipment tier (0+ - native items are 0-6, mods may add higher). A hero level appropriate for the tier.

MARK: WeaponPrefFromSkill
Derives weapon type preferences from a hero's combat skills. Analyzes the hero's skill values to determine optimal weapon loadout.
The hero to analyze. WeaponTypeFlags representing the hero's preferred weapon types.

MARK: WeaponFlagFromSkill
Determines weapon type flags based on sorted combat skills.
List of skill/value pairs sorted by descending value. Whether the hero is primarily mounted (affects polearm shield compatibility). WeaponTypeFlags combining primary and secondary weapon preferences.

MARK: HeroCombatSkillLvl
Gets all combat skill values for a hero.
The hero to analyze. List of skill/value pairs for all combat skills.

MARK: IsRangedSkill
Determines if a skill is a ranged combat skill.
The skill to check. True if the skill is Bow, Crossbow, or Throwing; false otherwise.

MARK: IsMeleeSkill
Determines if a skill is a melee combat skill.
The skill to check. True if the skill is OneHanded, TwoHanded, or Polearm; false otherwise.

MARK: RangedFlagsForSkill
Gets the weapon type flags corresponding to a ranged skill.
The ranged skill. Appropriate WeaponTypeFlags for the ranged skill.

MARK: MeleeFlagsForSkill
Gets the weapon type flags corresponding to a melee skill.
The melee skill. Appropriate WeaponTypeFlags for the melee skill.

MARK: SetFormationClass
Sets the hero's DefaultFormationClass based on their equipped items. Rules: - Mounted + melee primary -> Cavalry (HeavyCavalry if couching polearm + plate armor tier 5+) - Mounted + ranged primary -> HorseArcher - Unmounted + ranged primary -> Ranged - Unmounted + melee primary -> Infantry (HeavyInfantry if plate armor tier 5+) - 2 throwing stacks + no plate + unmounted -> Skirmisher - Fallback -> General
The hero whose formation class to set. The assigned FormationClass.

MARK: HasMountEquipped
Checks if equipment has a mount (horse) equipped.
The equipment to check. True if a mount is equipped; false otherwise.

MARK: GetPrimarySkillWithEquip
Gets the hero's primary weapon skill that has matching equipment. Returns the highest skill where the hero actually has a weapon of that type equipped.
The hero to analyze. The equipment to check. The skill object for the hero's primary weapon type.

MARK: HasWeaponForSkill
Checks if equipment contains a weapon matching the given skill.
The equipment to check. The skill to match against. True if a matching weapon is equipped; false otherwise.

MARK: HasPlateBodyArmor
Checks if body armor is plate material AND tier 5+.
The equipment to check. True if plate armor tier 5+ is equipped; false otherwise.

MARK: HasCouchingPolearm
Checks if equipment contains a couching polearm.
The equipment to check. True if a couching polearm is equipped; false otherwise.

MARK: CountThrowingStacks
Counts the number of throwing weapon stacks in equipment.
The equipment to check. The number of throwing weapon stacks equipped.

MARK: IsOneHandedMelee
Checks if item is a one-handed melee weapon (not polearm, not ranged).
The item to check. True if one-handed melee weapon; false otherwise.

MARK: IsTwoHandedMelee
Checks if item is a two-handed melee weapon (not polearm, not ranged).
The item to check. True if two-handed melee weapon; false otherwise.

MARK: SetDefaultFormationClass
Sets the DefaultFormationClass property on a BasicCharacterObject using reflection. The setter is protected, so direct access is not possible from external classes.
The character to modify. The formation class to set.

MARK: ClearEquipmentSlots
Clears all equipment slots by setting them to EquipmentElement.Invalid. This prevents template bleed-through where items from the source character template persist when no new item is found for a slot during equipment generation.
The equipment object to clear.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager
member.summary: Singleton manager that maintains categorized pools of game items for equipment generation. Pools are organized by culture, tier, weapon type, armor type, and other relevant categories.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.Instance
member.summary: Gets the singleton instance of the ItemPoolManager.

member.summary: Gets whether the item pools have been initialized.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.TotalItemsProcessed
member.summary: Gets the total number of items processed during initialization.

member.summary: Gets the number of valid items added to pools.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.WeaponPoolsByCulture
member.summary: Gets the weapon pools organized by culture, tier, and weapon type.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.NeutralWeaponPools
member.summary: Gets the neutral weapon pools (no culture restriction) organized by tier and weapon type.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.ArmorPoolsByCulture
member.summary: Gets the armor pools organized by culture, tier, and material type.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.ArmorPoolsBySlot
member.summary: Gets the armor pools organized by culture, tier, and equipment slot.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.NeutralArmorPools
member.summary: Gets the neutral armor pools (no culture restriction) organized by tier and slot.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.HorsePoolsByCulture
member.summary: Gets the horse pools organized by culture and tier.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.HarnessPoolsByCulture
member.summary: Gets the harness pools organized by culture and tier.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.NeutralHorsePools
member.summary: Gets the neutral horse pools organized by tier.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.NeutralHarnessPools
member.summary: Gets the neutral harness pools organized by tier.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.ShieldPoolsByCulture
member.summary: Gets the shield pools organized by culture and tier.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.NeutralShieldPools
member.summary: Gets the neutral shield pools organized by tier.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.AmmoPools
member.summary: Gets the ammunition pools organized by ammo type.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.BannerPoolsByCulture
member.summary: Gets the banner pools organized by culture.

member.name: P:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemPoolManager.NeutralBannerPool
member.summary: Gets the neutral banner pool.

members.member:
MARK: Initialize
Initializes or reinitializes all item pools by scanning game items. Call this after game data is fully loaded.

MARK: Clear
Clears all item pools and resets initialization state.

MARK: Reinitialize
Reinitializes the item pools by clearing and rebuilding them.
MARK: InitializePoolStructs

MARK: CategorizeAndAdd
Categorizes an item and adds it to the appropriate pool(s).

MARK: AddWeaponToPool
Adds a weapon to the cultural weapon pool.

MARK: AddWeaponNeutral
Adds a weapon to the neutral weapon pool (no culture).

MARK: AddShieldToPool
Adds a shield to the cultural shield pool.

MARK: AddShieldNeutral
Adds a shield to the neutral shield pool.

MARK: AddAmmoToPool
Adds ammunition to the ammo pool based on type.

MARK: AddArmorToPool
Adds armor to the cultural armor pools (by slot and by material).

MARK: AddArmorToSlotPool
Adds armor to the cultural armor pool organized by slot.

MARK: AddArmorToMatPool
Adds armor to the cultural armor pool organized by material type.

MARK: AddArmorNeutral
Adds armor to the neutral armor pool (no culture).

MARK: AddHorseToPool
Adds a horse to the cultural horse pool.

MARK: AddHorseNeutral
Adds a horse to the neutral horse pool.

MARK: AddHarnessToPool
Adds a harness to the cultural harness pool. Filters out pack animal harnesses (mule harnesses, bags, etc.) that are inappropriate for combat.

MARK: AddHarnessNeutral
Adds a harness to the neutral harness pool. Filters out pack animal harnesses (mule harnesses, bags, etc.) that are inappropriate for combat.

MARK: AddBannerToPool
Adds a banner to the cultural banner pool.

MARK: WeaponClassToFlag
Converts a native WeaponClass enum value to our WeaponTypeFlags enum.

MARK: GetArmorSlot
Gets the equipment slot for an armor item based on its item type.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation
member.summary: Provides static methods for validating items and determining their suitability for equipment pools and hero assignment based on various criteria.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.genderExceptionStringIds
member.summary: StringIds that should be except from gender check and is compatibile for all genders

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.FemaleOnlyKeywords
member.summary: Keywords that identify female-only items (case insensitive). Males cannot wear items containing these keywords in Name or StringId.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.BlacklistedItemNames
member.summary: Item names that should be completely excluded from equipment pools.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.BlacklistedKeywords
member.summary: Keywords that identify items to exclude from equipment pools.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.BlacklistedWeaponStringIds
member.summary: Specific weapon StringIds to exclude from equipment pools.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.CrownKeywords
member.summary: Keywords that identify crown items.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.SpecialCrownItems
member.summary: Special crown items identified by StringId that may not contain crown keyword.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.MinimumCivilianAppearance
member.summary: Minimum appearance value for standard civilian items. Items with Appearance > this value are considered suitable.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.MinimumRoyalAppearance
member.summary: Minimum appearance value for ruling clan members' civilian items. Items with Appearance > this value are considered suitable for royalty.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.MinimumBattleArmorAppearance
member.summary: Minimum appearance value for battle armor items. Items with Appearance > this value are considered suitable for battle.

members.member:
MARK: IsBlacklistedItem
Determines if an item is blacklisted and should be excluded from equipment pools. Checks both exact name matches and keyword matches.
The item to check. True if the item is blacklisted; false otherwise.

MARK: IsBlacklistedWeaponStringId
Determines if a weapon item is blacklisted by StringId. Checks for specific weapon StringIds that should be excluded.
The item to check. True if the weapon StringId is blacklisted; false otherwise.

MARK: IsValidItem
Determines if an item is valid for inclusion in equipment pools. Excludes blacklisted items, placeholder items (DP prefix), items with no functional stats, and other invalid items.
The item to validate. True if the item is valid for equipment pools; false otherwise.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.IsArmorSuitableForGender(TaleWorlds.Core.ItemObject,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Determines if an armor item is suitable for a hero based on gender. Females can wear anything; males cannot wear items with female-only keywords.
member.param: The armor item to check.
Whether the hero is female.
member.returns: True if the item is suitable for the specified gender; false otherwise.

members.member:
MARK: GetTierRangeForLevel
Gets the valid tier range for equipment based on hero level. Higher level heroes use higher tier equipment with appropriate minimum tiers. Tier ranges are calibrated so heroes get level-appropriate gear: - Level 0-9: Tier 1-3 (basic equipment) - Level 10-14: Tier 3-4 (improved equipment) - Level 15-19: Tier 4-5 (quality equipment) - Level 20-24: Tier 5-6 (elite equipment) - Level 25+: Tier 6 (legendary equipment)
The hero's level. A tuple containing the minimum and maximum allowed item tiers.

MARK: IsItemInTierRange
Determines if an item's tier falls within the specified tier range.
The item to check. The minimum allowed tier. The maximum allowed tier. True if the item's tier is within the range; false otherwise.

MARK: ShouldExcludeCloth
Determines if cloth armor should be excluded for a hero based on level and equipment slot. Low-level heroes (under 10) can wear cloth armor. Capes are always allowed to be cloth.
The hero's level. The equipment slot being filled. True if cloth armor should be excluded; false if cloth is allowed.

MARK: IsClothArmor
Determines if an item is cloth armor based on its armor component material type.
The item to check. True if the item is cloth armor; false otherwise.

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.CivilianKeywords
member.summary: Keywords that identify items as civilian appropriate (even without IsCivilian flag).

member.name: F:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemValidation.CombatOnlyKeywords
member.summary: Keywords that identify items as combat-specific (NOT civilian appropriate).

members.member:
MARK: IsCivilianAppropriateItem
Determines if an item is suitable for civilian equipment. Civilian appropriate items include: - Items explicitly marked as IsCivilian by the game - Dresses, ladies shoes, civilian crowns - Cloth and leather items without combat keywords - Items with civilian keywords (tunic, shirt, pants, etc.) Excludes items with combat keywords (armor, mail, helm, etc.)
The item to check. True if the item is suitable for civilian equipment; false otherwise.

MARK: IsDressItem
Determines if an item is a dress based on name or StringId containing "dress".
The item to check. True if the item is a dress; false otherwise.

MARK: IsLadiesShoes
Determines if an item is ladies shoes based on name containing "Ladies Shoes" or "Lady Shoes".
The item to check. True if the item is ladies shoes; false otherwise.

MARK: IsCrownItem
Determines if an item is a crown based on crown keywords or special crown item identifiers.
The item to check. True if the item is a crown; false otherwise.

MARK: IsBattleCrown
Determines if an item is a battle crown (crown with "battle" or "helmet" in name). Battle crowns are suitable for combat equipment.
The item to check. True if the item is a battle crown; false otherwise.

MARK: IsCivilianCrown
Determines if an item is a civilian crown (crown without "battle" or "helm" in name). Civilian crowns are suitable for non-combat equipment.
The item to check. True if the item is a civilian crown; false otherwise.

MARK: IsJeweledCrown
Determines if an item is a jeweled crown based on name containing "Jeweled Crown".
The item to check. True if the item is a jeweled crown; false otherwise.

MARK: IsPolearmWeapon
Determines if a weapon item is a polearm (including one-handed polearms). Polearms always require a sidearm weapon for backup melee combat.
The weapon item to check. True if the item is a polearm; false otherwise.

MARK: IsSidearmWeapon
Determines if a weapon item is a sidearm (one-handed sword, axe, mace, or dagger). Sidearms are backup weapons used when polearm is not effective (close combat).
The weapon item to check. True if the item is a sidearm; false otherwise.

MARK: IsRulingClanMember
Determines if a hero is a member of the ruling clan of their kingdom.
The hero to check. True if the hero is in the ruling clan of their kingdom; false otherwise.

MARK: MeetsAppearanceThreshold
Checks if an item meets the specified appearance threshold.
The item to check. The minimum appearance value (exclusive). True if item.Appearance > minAppearance; false otherwise.

MARK: MeetsCivilianAppearance
Checks if an item meets civilian appearance requirements based on hero status.
The item to check. Whether the hero is a member of a ruling clan. True if item meets appearance requirements; false otherwise.

MARK: MeetsBattleArmorAppearance
Checks if an armor item meets battle appearance requirements.
The item to check. True if item.Appearance > MinimumBattleArmorAppearance; false otherwise.

MARK: IsValidWeapon
Validates a weapon item by checking for damage stats. Shields use BodyArmor stat instead of damage stats.
The weapon item to validate. True if the weapon has valid stats; false otherwise.

MARK: IsValidArmor
Validates an armor item by checking for armor stats. At least one armor value must be greater than zero.
The armor item to validate. True if the armor has valid stats; false otherwise.

MARK: IsValidHorse
Validates a horse item by checking for horse stats. At least one stat (Speed, Maneuver, ChargeDamage) must be greater than zero.
The horse item to validate. True if the horse has valid stats; false otherwise.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.MountCompatibility
member.summary: Provides static methods for determining weapon and item compatibility with mounted combat. Used by the HeroOutfitter system when generating equipment for mounted heroes.

members.member:
MARK: IsCouchablePolearm
Determines if a polearm can be couched while mounted. Uses native detection: WeaponDescriptionId contains "couch". Couchable polearms are designed for mounted combat.
The polearm item to check. True if the polearm can be couched; false otherwise.

MARK: IsBraceablePolearm
Determines if a polearm can be braced (infantry anti-cavalry). Uses native detection: WeaponDescriptionId contains "bracing". Braceable polearms (pikes) are infantry-only weapons.
The polearm item to check. True if the polearm can be braced; false otherwise.

MARK: IsPolearmUsableOnMount
Determines if a polearm can be used effectively while mounted. Braceable polearms (pikes) are infantry-only and should NOT be given to mounted heroes. Couchable polearms are explicitly designed for mounted combat.
The polearm item to check. True if the polearm can be used on a mount; false otherwise.

MARK: IsWepUsableOnMount
Determines if a weapon can be used while mounted on a horse. Uses native ItemUsageSetFlags check via MBItem.GetItemUsageSetFlags(). Also respects CantReloadOnHorseback flag for crossbows.
The weapon item to check. True if the weapon can be used on a mount; false otherwise.

MARK: IsWepUsableOnMount Pole
Determines if a weapon is usable on mount considering weapon type-specific exceptions. Uses native ItemUsageSetFlags and polearm-specific checks.
The weapon item to check. Whether to allow polearms (uses IsPolearmUsableOnMount for filtering). True if the weapon can be used on a mount; false otherwise.

MARK: IsMountOrHarness
Determines if an item is a mount (horse) or harness (horse armor).
The item to check. True if the item is a horse or horse harness; false otherwise.

MARK: IsMount
Determines if an item is specifically a mount (horse, not harness).
The item to check. True if the item is a horse; false otherwise.

MARK: IsHarness
Determines if an item is specifically a harness (horse armor, not horse).
The item to check. True if the item is a horse harness; false otherwise.

MARK: IsCombatHarness
Determines if a harness is appropriate for combat use (not a pack animal harness). Filters out items designed for mules and pack animals like "Mule Harness with Bags".
The harness item to check. True if the harness is combat-appropriate; false if it's a pack animal harness.

MARK: HasWeaponFlag
Checks if a weapon component has a specific weapon flag. Uses HasAnyFlag extension method pattern for compatibility.
The weapon component data to check. The weapon flag to check for. True if the weapon has the specified flag; false otherwise.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.Inventory.InventoryManager
member.summary:
High-level API for opening the native inventory UI with any hero, party, or custom rosters. Supports opening inventory for heroes from ANY clan by using synthetic TroopRosters that bypass the native CanSelectHero() player clan restriction.
The native inventory system uses:
- ItemRoster for item storage on each side
- TroopRoster for hero character switching arrows
- InventoryLogic for transfer/trade logic
- InventoryState (native GameState) + GauntletInventoryScreen (auto-registered)

members.member:
MARK: Hero Only
Creates an inventory manager for a single hero with discard panel on the left. The hero is displayed in the middle with full equipment editing. Works for heroes from ANY clan.
Hero whose equipment and party inventory to display

MARK: Hero vs Hero
Creates an inventory manager with two heroes' party inventories side by side. The middleHero is displayed in the center equipment panel. Both heroes' party items appear on their respective sides.
Hero whose party appears on the right side Hero whose party appears on the left side Hero shown in the middle equipment view (defaults to leftHero)

MARK: Party vs Party
Creates an inventory manager with two mobile parties side by side. Party member rosters are used for hero switching arrows. The right party leader is shown in the middle initially.
Party shown on the right side Party shown on the left side

MARK: Custom Rosters
Creates an inventory manager with fully custom item and hero rosters. Maximum flexibility for advanced use cases such as custom stashes, loot screens, or other non-standard inventory configurations.
Items shown on the left panel Items shown on the right panel Heroes available for switching on the right side Label for the left panel Heroes available for switching on the left side (optional)

MARK: OpenInventory
Opens the native inventory UI based on the constructor configuration. The native GauntletInventoryScreen auto-registers for InventoryState, so no custom GameState or ScreenBase is required.
Optional callback when inventory closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.Inventory.InventoryManager.InventoryOpenMode
member.summary: Defines the mode of opening the inventory, determined by which constructor was used.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.Inventory.InventoryUILauncher
member.summary: Static launcher responsible for creating InventoryState + InventoryLogic, wiring them together, and pushing the state to open the native inventory UI. Supports opening inventory for ANY hero from ANY clan by using synthetic TroopRosters.

members.member:
MARK: OpenHeroInventory
Opens the inventory UI for a single hero with an empty discard panel on the left. The hero is shown in the middle with full equipment editing regardless of clan. Uses a synthetic TroopRoster so no clan restrictions apply.
The hero whose equipment to display in the middle Optional callback when inventory closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: OpenHeroToHeroInventory
Opens the inventory UI with two heroes' party inventories side by side. The middleHero is displayed in the center equipment panel regardless of clan. Both parties' items are shown on their respective sides.
Hero whose party appears on the right side Hero whose party appears on the left side Hero shown in the middle equipment panel (defaults to rightHero if null) Optional callback when inventory closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: OpenPartyToPartyInventory
Opens the inventory UI with two parties side by side. Uses party member rosters for hero switching arrows. The right party leader is shown in the middle initially.
Party shown on the right side Party shown on the left side Optional callback when inventory closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: OpenWithCustomRosters
Opens the inventory UI with fully custom item rosters and hero rosters. Maximum flexibility for advanced use cases.
Items shown on the left panel Items shown on the right panel Heroes available for switching on the right side Hero shown in the middle equipment panel The owner party for the right side (can be null) Label displayed on the left panel Heroes available for switching on the left side (optional) Whether to enable buy/sell pricing Inventory mode (Default, Trade, Loot, Stash, Warehouse) Optional callback when inventory closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: PushInventoryState
Creates an InventoryState, assigns the logic and delegates, and pushes it. The native GauntletInventoryScreen auto-registers for InventoryState.

MARK: CreateSyntheticHeroRoster
Creates a synthetic TroopRoster containing only the specified hero. This bypasses the CanSelectHero() clan check in SPInventoryVM because the hero is set as InitialEquipmentCharacter BEFORE CanSelectHero runs, and CanSelectHero only affects the character switching arrows, not the initial middle character display or equipment editing capability.

MARK: GetMarketData
Gets market data for pricing. Uses current settlement if available, nearest town otherwise, or falls back to DefaultMarketData (base item values). Mirrors the native InventoryScreenHelper.GetCurrentMarketDataForPlayer() pattern.

member.summary:
Finds an item with the specified itemId. Fast but case sensitive (all string ids SHOULD be lower case)

Example: QueryItems("iron_sword").FirstOrDefault()

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemQueries.QueryItems(System.String,Bannerlord.GameMaster.Items.ItemTypes,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.String,System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Performance focused method to find items matching multiple parameters. All parameters are optional and can be used with none, one or a combination of any parameters
Note: parameter is a string used that will match partial item names or partial stringIds
member.param: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter by name or ID
Item type flags that ALL must match (AND logic)
If true, item must have ALL flags. If false, item must have ANY flag
Optional tier filter (0-6, -1 for no filter)
Optional culture StringId filter
Optional civilian filter (null = any, true = civilian, false = battle)
Sort field (id, name, tier, value, type, culture, loadout, or any ItemType flag)
True for descending, false for ascending
member.returns: List of items matching all criteria

members.member:
MARK: PropagateRulingClanBanner
Propagates the ruling clan's banner colors to this kingdom and all vassal clans. Should be called after modifying a ruling clan's banner to ensure consistency. Uses reflection to set Kingdom.PrimaryBannerColor (banner background) and Kingdom.SecondaryBannerColor (icon color), then triggers banner color updates on all kingdom clans. Note: Native game only uses primary (background) and icon colors for kingdom propagation - secondary background is NOT used.
The kingdom to propagate banner colors to. BLGMResult indicating success or failure.

MARK: SetBannerColors
Sets this kingdom's color properties via reflection (all have private setters, no public alternative). Sets PrimaryBannerColor and SecondaryBannerColor (used by native UpdateBannerColorsAccordingToKingdom to propagate to clan banners), and Color/Color2 (used by map renderer for cloth tinting via party.MapFaction.Color/Color2). Does NOT mutate the kingdom Banner object directly -- actual banner visual data is updated per-clan via UpdateBannerColorsForKingdom() which calls ChangePrimaryColor/ChangeIconColors on each clan's banner. Mutating kingdom.Banner directly here is destructive because ChangePrimaryColor sets both ColorId AND ColorId2 to the same value, wiping distinct secondary background colors, and can silently fail if BannerManager.GetColorId returns -1.
The kingdom to set banner colors for. The primary banner color (background). The secondary banner color (icon). BLGMResult indicating success or failure.

MARK: Wrapper
Wrapper class implementing IEntityExtensions interface for Kingdom entities

MARK: Create Kingdom
Creates a new kingdom with the specified ruling clan and home settlement.
Capital settlement (auto-resolves if null/invalid) Kingdom name (generates random if null) Name of the Clan ruling to rule the kingdom (generates random if null) The created kingdom, or null if settlement cannot be resolved

MARK: Prepare Clan for Rule
Make sure clan is ready to be a ruling clan
Leaves kingdom if in one, forces correct state, sets tier to 6, add gold and influence, add 10 lords to clan

MARK: Generate Kingdoms
Generates multiple kingdoms by taking settlements from existing kingdoms. Alternates between kingdoms evenly, ensuring not to take a kingdom's last settlement. Does not take settlements from the player's kingdom.
Number of kingdoms to create Number of vassal clans per kingdom Culture pool for kingdoms List of created kingdoms

MARK: InitializeRelations
Initialize Relations to trigger more war, weighted torwards negative relations

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Party.PartyManager
member.summary:
High-level API for opening the native party screen UI with any hero or party. Provides convenience methods that resolve heroes to their parties and delegate to PartyUILauncher for the actual native screen wiring.
The native party system uses:
- TroopRoster for member and prisoner rosters on each side
- PartyScreenLogic for troop transfer, upgrade, and recruitment logic
- PartyState (native GameState) + GauntletPartyScreen (auto-registered)
Two modes are supported:
- Discard mode: Right side is a real party, left side has all game troops (100 each)
- Party-to-Party mode: Two real parties side by side for troop exchange

members.member:
MARK: Hero + Hero
Opens the party editor with two heroes' parties side by side. If leftSideHero is null, same party as rightSideHero, or has no party, falls back to discard mode with all game troops on the left.
Hero whose party appears on the right side (required) Hero whose party appears on the left side (null for discard mode) Optional callback when party screen closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: Hero Only
Opens the party editor for a single hero's party with all game troops on the left (discard mode).
Hero whose party to edit (required) Optional callback when party screen closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: Party + Party
Opens the party editor with two mobile parties side by side for troop transfer. If leftSideParty is null or the same as rightSideParty, falls back to discard mode.
Party shown on the right side (required) Party shown on the left side (null for discard mode) Optional callback when party screen closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: Party Only
Opens the party editor for a single party with all game troops on the left (discard mode).
Party to edit (required) Optional callback when party screen closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Party.PartyUILauncher
member.summary:
Static launcher responsible for creating PartyScreenLogic + PartyScreenLogicInitializationData, wiring them together, and pushing the PartyState to open the native party screen UI. The native GauntletPartyScreen auto-registers for PartyState via [GameStateScreen(typeof(PartyState))].
Supports two modes:
- Party-to-Party: Two real parties side by side with full troop transfer
- Discard/All Troops: Right side is a real party, left side shows all game troops (100 each)
Party leaders and the player character are never transferable.
Heroes discarded in discard mode are set to Fugitive state so they re-enter the game world.

members.member:
MARK: OpenPartyToParty
Opens the party screen with two real parties side by side for troop transfer. Both member and prisoner rosters are available for transfer. Blocks the player character and both party leaders from being transferred.
Party shown on the right side Party shown on the left side Optional callback when party screen closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: OpenWithDiscardRoster
Opens the party screen with the right side showing a real party and the left side populated with all game troops (100 of each), similar to the native cheat mode pattern. The left side acts as a discard/source roster for adding troops to the party.
Heroes removed from the right party (discarded) are set to Fugitive state via MakeHeroFugitiveAction so the game's TeleportationCampaignBehavior can re-place them at a nearby settlement. Blocks the player character and party leader from transfer.
Party shown on the right side Optional callback when party screen closes BLGMResult indicating success or failure

MARK: SnapshotRosterHeroes
Creates a snapshot of all hero objects currently in a troop roster. Used to detect which heroes were removed when the party screen closes.

MARK: ApplyFugitiveState
Compares initial roster heroes against the final right roster after the party screen closes. Any hero that was in the initial roster but not in the final roster was discarded, and gets MakeHeroFugitiveAction applied so the game's TeleportationCampaignBehavior will eventually re-place them at a nearby settlement. Skips the player character as a safety check.

MARK: GetAllTroopsRoster
Creates a TroopRoster containing all valid encyclopedia troops with TroopCountPerType (100) of each. Mirrors the native GetRosterWithAllGameTroops() pattern but uses a higher count. Troops are sorted alphabetically by name.

MARK: PushPartyState
Creates a PartyState, assigns the logic and configuration, and pushes it. The native GauntletPartyScreen auto-registers for PartyState.

MARK: DefaultDoneHandler
Default handler for the Done button that simply accepts all changes. The native PartyScreenLogic applies roster changes automatically when Done returns true.

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.SettlementDistanceHelpers
member.summary: Provides useful methods for calculating map distance, travel distance and travel time considering pathing and terrain Allows calculations for different navigation types (Land, Naval, All)

members.member:
MARK: Distance Calculation
Finds the nearest Town, Castle, or Village to the specified settlement
The settlement to use to find the next closest settlement. Will not return the input settlement

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.SettlementDistanceHelpers.FindNearestNonHideoutSettlement(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.CampaignVec2,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Finds the nearest Town, Castle, or Village. By default, if the position is the position of a settlement already, that settlement will be returned Use ignoreSettlementsAtInputPosition = true to if you would rather return the next closest settlement.
member.param: The campaign map position to find the closest settlement to

If true will skip settlements that have the same position within tolerance of 0.1fas the input position The settlement position, gateposition, and port position will be checked against the tolerance if true

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.SettlementDistanceHelpers.FindNearestNonHideoutSettlementByPath(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement,TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Party.MobileParty.NavigationType)
member.summary: Finds the nearest Town, Castle, or Village using actual pathing distance (considers terrain, obstacles). Slower than FindNearestNonHideoutSettlement but accounts for rivers, mountains, etc.
member.param: The source settlement
Navigation capability: Default (land), Naval (sea), or All (both)
member.returns: Nearest settlement by pathing distance, or null if none found

members.member:
MARK: ChangeOwner
Sets new owner of the settlement by calling ChangeOwnerOfSettlementAction

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.SettlementManager.GetCultureBehavior
member.summary: Gets the SettlementCultureBehavior instance from the current campaign.

members.member:
MARK: Change Culture
Changes the culture of a settlement with persistence through save/load cycles. Routes through SettlementCultureBehavior for automatic persistence tracking.
The settlement to change culture for The new culture to apply If true, updates the culture of all notables in the settlement If true and settlement is a town/castle, recursively updates bound villages BLGMResult indicating success or failure with a message

MARK: Set Culture
Changes the culture of a settlement with persistence through save/load cycles. This is a compatibility overload. Prefer ChangeSettlementCulture() for richer BLGMResult error handling.
The settlement to change culture for The new culture to apply If true, updates the culture of all notables in the settlement If true and settlement is a town/castle, recursively updates bound villages True if the culture change was successful, false otherwise

MARK: Has Custom Culture
Checks if a settlement has a custom culture set by GameMaster.
The settlement to check True if the settlement has a custom culture

MARK: Get Original Culture
Gets the original culture of a settlement before it was changed by GameMaster.
The settlement to check Original culture if changed, null otherwise

MARK: Reset Culture
Resets a settlement to its original culture.
The settlement to reset BLGMResult indicating success or failure with a message

MARK: Custom Culture Count
Gets the number of settlements with custom cultures.
The count of settlements with custom cultures

MARK: Bound Village Count
Gets the count of bound villages for a settlement.
The settlement to check The number of bound villages, or 0 if none or settlement is null

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.SettlementQueries
member.summary: Provides query methods for searching, finding, filtering, or sorting settlement entities.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.SettlementQueries.GetSettlementById(System.String)
member.summary:
Finds a settlement with the specified settlementId, Fast but case sensitive (all string ids SHOULD be lower case)
Use QuerySettlements().FirstOrDefault() to find settlement with case insensitive partial name or partial stringIds
Example: QuerySettlements("Maracanda").FirstOrDefault()

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.SettlementQueries.QuerySettlements(System.String,Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.SettlementTypes,System.Boolean,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Performance focused method to find settlements matching multiple parameters. All parameters are optional and can be used with none, one or a combination of any parameters
Note: parameter is a string used that will match partial settlement names or partial stringIds
member.param: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter by name or ID
Settlement type flags that ALL must match (AND logic)
If true, settlement must have ALL flags. If false, settlement must have ANY flag
Sort field (id, name, type, owner, kingdom, culture, prosperity, or any SettlementType flag)
True for descending, false for ascending
member.returns: List of settlements matching all criteria
