member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.VillageExtensions.GetRecommendedTradeBound(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Village)
member.summary: Finds the best trade-bound town for a village using the game's native algorithm. Prefers same-faction towns, falls back to neutral/allied towns within travel distance.
member.param: Village to find trade town for
member.returns: Best town for trade, or null if none found within reasonable distance

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.VillageExtensions.SetBoundSettlement(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Village,TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement)
member.summary:
Attempts to Change which settlement (castle/town) a village is bound to.
If the bound settlement is a town, the village is also tradebound to the town as the game will ignore the tradebound anyway for town bound villages
If the bound settlment is a castle, the village will automatically be trade bound to a town recommened by native bannerlord, as castles dont have trade markets
Changes persist automatically due to [SaveableField] attributes.
member.returns: BLGMResult containing a bool wasSuccessful, and a string message

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.VillageExtensions.SetTradeBoundSettlement(TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Village,TaleWorlds.CampaignSystem.Settlements.Settlement)
member.summary:
Attempts to change which settlement (must be town not castle) the village is TradeBound to
If the village is already regular bound to a town, then it ignores the trade bound settlement and should be set the same as the regular binding
If the village is regular bound to a castle, then it needs to be trade bound to a town as castes dont have trade markets. (SetBoundSettlment() tries to do this automatically)
member.returns: BLGMResult containing a bool wasSuccessful, and a string message

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Settlements.VillageManager
member.summary:
Manages Villages for BLGM actions.
Remaining Village logic should be refactored out of commands to here or similar classes

members.member:
MARK: ApplicationTick
Executes every frame
Delta Time: Time since last frame

MARK: CreateMainMenuBtn
Adds a "Game Master" button to main menu of game.

MARK: MenuOptionClicked
Runs when the "Game Master" button is clicked in main menu.

MARK: ShowDisabledMsg
Evaluates whether the message option button should be disabled and provides the reason for its state.
A tuple containing a Boolean value indicating whether the button is disabled, and a describing the reason. If the button is enabled, the reason will be empty.

MARK: RegisterCommands
Register custom commands

MARK: GetCustomCommands
Get custom commands

MARK: Execute
Executes custom command if it exists, returns command not found if it doesnt exist

MARK: Clear
Clears system console

MARK: CloseConsole
Clears system console

MARK: CloseConsole
Clears system console

MARK: ListCommand
Lists commands using fuzzy search
MARK: Cheat Mode

MARK: QuitGame
Completely exits game

MARK: CollectCommands
Gets all registered .NET game console commands using reflection such as campaign. gm. Native c++ engine commands will not be found such as config. commands

MARK: GetCommands
returns cached list of game registered commands. Collects via reflection if not yet collected

MARK: GetAllCommands
returns cached list of all commands. Collects via reflection if not yet collected

MARK: FuzzySearch
Gets commands or groups matching up to next dot

MARK: CommonPrefix
Helper to find common prefix for auto-completion

member.name: T:Bannerlord.GameMaster.SystemConsoleManager
member.summary: Manages System Console, background thread, and input allowing commands to be executed from the system console

members.member: MARK: Properties

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.SystemConsoleManager.AllocConsole
member.summary: Allocates a new console for the calling process.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.SystemConsoleManager.FreeConsole
member.summary: Detaches the calling process from its console.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.SystemConsoleManager.GetConsoleWindow
member.summary: Retrieves the window handle used by the console associated with the calling process.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.SystemConsoleManager.ShowWindow(System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the specified window's show state.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.SystemConsoleManager.GetSystemMenu(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Allows the application to access the window menu for copying and modifying.

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.SystemConsoleManager.DeleteMenu(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Deletes an item from the specified menu.

members.member:
MARK: WriteStyledPrompt
Writes the colorized BLGM prompt and sets the color for user input

MARK: WriteLine
Writes to the console without mixing jumbling text but does not redraw input prompt

MARK: WriteLog
Writes to the console without interupting input if system console is enabled

MARK: ShowOnLaunch
Open and attach System Console if bannerlord was launched with "/systemconsole"

MARK: ApplyConsoleSettings
Sets the title and resizing the console window

MARK: ShowConsole
Open and attach System Console for debugging, or to show command output or error output

MARK: DisableCloseButton
Removes the Close (X) button from the System Menu to prevent accidental process termination.

MARK: ResetConsoleStreams
Forces .NET to refresh internal handles for In/Out. Required because System.Console caches handles that become invalid after FreeConsole().

MARK: Close Console
Hides the console window instead of freeing it. This prevents breaking Input Handles.

MARK: StartInputThread
Starts background thread listening for console input

MARK: ReadInput
Listens to console input in background without stalling game thread

MARK: ProcessKey
Processes a specific key press event and handles buffer manipulation

MARK: RedrawInputLine
Redraws the input buffer on the console, used after editing actions like backspace or history navigation

MARK: HandleHistory
Cycles through command history

MARK: TabCompletion
Handles tab key presses to trigger autocomplete or display suggestions

MARK: HandleInput
Checks console input and executes action if match found

MARK: Execute
Executes command on main thread

MARK: Enqueue
Add actions to the queue to be executed on the main thread

MARK: OnTick
Process queued commands on main thread

MARK: Router
Routes all standard Console.WriteLine calls to ThreadSafe WriteLog

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Troops.TroopQueries.GetTroopById(System.String)
member.summary:
Finds a troop with the specified troopId, Fast but case sensitive (all string ids SHOULD be lower case)
Use QueryTroops().FirstOrDefault() to find troop with case insensitive partial name or partial stringIds
Example: QueryTroops("henry").FirstOrDefault()

member.name: M:Bannerlord.GameMaster.Troops.TroopQueries.QueryTroops(System.String,Bannerlord.GameMaster.Troops.TroopTypes,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Performance focused method to find troops matching multiple parameters. All parameters are optional and can be used with none, one or a combination of any parameters
Note: parameter is a string used that will match partial troop names or partial stringIds CRITICAL: This method only returns actual combat troops (via IsActualTroop check), excluding NPCs, templates, and other non-troops.
member.param: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter by name or ID
Troop type flags that ALL must match (AND logic)
If true, troop must have ALL flags. If false, troop must have ANY flag
Optional tier filter (0-6+, -1 for no filter)
Sort field (id, name, tier, level, culture, formation, or any TroopTypes flag)
True for descending, false for ascending
member.returns: List of troops matching all criteria

member.summary: Upgrades all troops in the roster to the specified tier while maintaining desired composition ratios. When troops have multiple upgrade paths, intelligently splits them to achieve target ratios. Pre-analyzes all upgrade paths to account for troops with limited options.

Maximum tier to upgrade to (default: max tier)
Desired ratio of ranged troops (0.0-1.0, null for auto)
Desired ratio of cavalry troops (0.0-1.0, null for auto)
Desired ratio of infantry troops (0.0-1.0, null for auto)
